Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Policy Guidelines Objective
Nội dung chính: [Policy Guidelines Objective]
Each Jardine Matheson Group company should establish an Information Security Policy to provide
guidance over the governance and protection of the company’s information assets against events that
could compromise their confidentiality, integrity or availability. This document provides a framework for
all Jardine Matheson Group companies (hereafter referred to as the companies) to compile their
Information Security Policy. All Jardine Matheson Group companies should include their mission / vision
statements from their management when documenting their Information Security Policy. The security
areas and respective policy statements should be used as a basis for tailoring the company’s Information
Security Policy, standards and procedures.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Policy Guidelines Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Scope
Nội dung chính: [Scope]
This document applies to all companies under the Jardine Matheson Group.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Requirement Levels
Nội dung chính: [Requirement Levels]
Different tags have been used to indicate the force of the policy statements that are put forward:
• Policy statements tagged with [Baseline] are minimum requirements that the companies must
follow.
• Policy statements tagged with [Advanced] are requirements that the companies should adopt to
further strengthen their security posture. The companies should evaluate their risk exposure and
magnitude based on their business size, nature, amount of data etc. and determine which
“Advanced” requirements they need to adopt. Such requirements can encourage and help the
companies to achieve an even higher level of information security.
• Policy statements tagged otherwise (i.e. [PCI], [GDPR], [PDPO], [LGPD], [PIPL], [DSL], [PDP Law],
[CPRA], [CCPR]) are regulatory requirements applicable to the companies operating in certain
regions or industries. The companies should observe applicable laws and regulations and decide
whether they need to follow such requirements.
o [PCI] - Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
o [GDPR] – General Data Protection Regulation
o [PDPO] – Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (Cap. 486) of Hong Kong
o [LGPD] – Brazilian Data Protection Law
o [PIPL] – Personal Information Protection Law of China
o [DSL] – Data Security Law of China
o [PDP Law] – Personal Data Protection Law of Indonesia

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Requirement Levels

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 4
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 4]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 4

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
o [CPRA] – The California Privacy Rights Act of the United States
• Businesses should comply with all legal and regulatory requirement. If there are any conflict
between legal and regulatory requirement and this policy guidelines, legal and/or regulatory
requirement shall prevail. If there are any similar requirement between legal and regulatory
requirement and this policy guidelines, the more stringent requirement shall be followed.
Legally permissible and appropriate disciplinary actions up to and including termination of employment
shall be considered for staff who have violated the requirements listed within the company’s Information
Security Policy.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Policy Issuance
Nội dung chính: [Policy Issuance]
A copy of this document will be made available to every employee and will be displayed on the company
intranet.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Policy Issuance

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Policy Guidelines Review and Update
Nội dung chính: [Policy Guidelines Review and Update]
Updates to this document shall be made whenever there is any major business change, organisational
change or technology change which can alter the Jardine Matheson Group’s information security risk
posture. Moreover, this document shall be reviewed at least annually to make improvements and
clarifications so as to uphold accuracy, completeness, and to ensure sustained compliance with different
regulations.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Policy Guidelines Review and Update

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Exceptions to this Policy Guidelines
Nội dung chính: [Exceptions to this Policy Guidelines]
Exceptions to this Information Security Policy Guidelines (i.e., baseline requirements that could not be
adopted) require a formal request with written justifications to be approved by the company’s senior
management. Such request should also be submitted to the Group Cyber Security for approval. Approved
exceptions should be reviewed annually and recertified with Group Cyber Security’s approval.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Exceptions to this Policy Guidelines

content:
Ngữ cảnh: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES
Nội dung chính: [INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES]

metadata.hierarchy: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES

content:
Ngữ cảnh: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > Organisational Structure
Nội dung chính: [Organisational Structure]
The structure of relevant committees governing and upholding the company’s information security is
shown below:

metadata.hierarchy: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > Organisational Structure

content:
Ngữ cảnh: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > IT Steering Committee (ITSC)
Nội dung chính: [IT Steering Committee (ITSC)]
Information security is addressed at the ITSC Meetings between senior business and IT management to
ensure that there is clear direction and visible management support for security initiatives. The ITSC has
the following information security responsibilities:
• Minimising company information asset exposure
• Embedding information security within the company information planning process
• Approving the information security policy
• Coordinating policy implementation across the company
• Promoting information security awareness
• Reviewing serious information security incidents and enhancing policies and procedures as
necessary
• Approving major initiatives to enhance information security

metadata.hierarchy: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > IT Steering Committee (ITSC)

content:
Ngữ cảnh: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > Risk Management Committee (RMC)
Nội dung chính: [Risk Management Committee (RMC)]
Risk Management is the means by which the company identify, assess and control threats to its assets,
personnel, capital and earnings. The risk management program comprises three key elements:
• Business continuity planning (BCP) and disaster recovery (DR)
• Insurance
• Compliance with information security policy and other mandatory and recommended standards

metadata.hierarchy: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > Risk Management Committee (RMC)

content:
Ngữ cảnh: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 6
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 6]

metadata.hierarchy: INFORMATION SECURITY RESPONSIBILITIES > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 6

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
Risk management is the statutory responsibility of the Board of Directors who oversee the company’s risk
management policies and practices and, in turn, delegate responsibilities to the RMC.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Security Committee (ISC)
Nội dung chính: [Information Security Committee (ISC)]
The ISC acts on behalf of the company’s senior management and is responsible for setting the overall
direction of information security. This direction should be consistent with previously agreed company
information security policies, procedures, standards and guidelines.
The ISC is responsible for:
• Monitoring procedures and tools used to assess risks, priorities and cost/benefits of implementing
security
• Monitoring any issues raised by internal or external security audits and ensuring their satisfactory
resolution
• Monitoring the incident log
• Performing an overall high level risk assessment of the potential threats to the business and the
adequacy of existing controls
• Monitoring the business’s obligation to comply with external information security standards
• Identifying business information and assigning Information Owners
• Reviewing and approving information classifications by Information Owners
• Determining whether existing security controls are commensurate with the information
classifications
• Reporting any suspected security breaches to ITSC and RMC
On an annual basis, the ISC should review the existing information security policy document with a view
to making any recommendations for improvement to ITSC for approval and incorporation into the next
release of the information security policy document.
The ISC should comprise:
• Head of Information Technology
• Head of Internal Audit
• Head of Finance
• Information Security Officer(s)
• Risk Management
• A representative from Human Resources
• Senior management and Information Owners from each key operational department of function
within the company
Note: Business units may integrate the function and responsibilities of the ISC with the ITSC if their scale
does not require the operation of separate committees.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Security Committee (ISC)

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Security Officer
Nội dung chính: [Information Security Officer]
Each company or business unit should appoint an information security officer who is responsible for:

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Security Officer

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 7
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 7]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 7

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
• Recommending strategies for the protection of information assets according to the company’s
strategic business needs
• Working with business executive to implement a process to identify threats to the organisation’s
information assets and computing resources
• Assisting Information Owners to identify and implement controls to mitigate against the threats
to information assets and computing resources
• Implementing new security awareness programs
• Sharing new threat or incident information with other information security officers as necessary

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Owner
Nội dung chính: [Information Owner]
The Information Owner is responsible for:
• Liaising with business management to develop and enhance the policy under their control
• Ensuring the information under their control is secured as effectively as practically possible
• Classifying information under their care
• Authorizing access to those who have a business need for the information
• Removing access (or modifying it) from those who no longer have a business need for the
information
• Communicating access control requirements to IT management and the information users
• Monitoring policy compliance with their scope of authority
• Responding to any detected security incident and ensuring it is reported
Information Owners may delegate their security responsibilities to other team members or service
providers, but they remain accountable for ensuring that adequate security is implemented to fulfil their
responsibilities.
To avoid any misunderstanding about respective Information Owner responsibilities:
• The information and associated access within each system should be identified and defined
• The person responsible for the information or authorizing access should be agreed and
responsibility documented
• Authorisation levels should be defined and documented

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Owner

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Custodian
Nội dung chính: [Information Custodian]
An information custodian is an employee, vendor, contractor, or other authorised person who has the
responsibility for maintaining and/or supporting corporate information. Information custodians are
delegated this responsibility by Information Owners. System and security administrators who work under
information custodians are personnel designated to maintain, operate and implement technology
solutions for the relevant Information Owners concerned. To avoid any misunderstanding about
respective Information Owner responsibilities, their responsibilities may include:

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Information Custodian

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 8
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 8]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 8

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
• Security maintenance of systems, including application of patches to operating systems and
applications
• System documentation
• System performance monitoring
• Receiving security alerts generated by systems, and proactively monitoring systems for security
incidents
• Responding to security alerts according to documented process, escalating where necessary to
the information security officer, to resolve incidents
• Implementation of technical security controls, including system hardening and configuration
• Communication with the information security officer about security-related incidents and issues

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > User Responsibilities
Nội dung chính: [User Responsibilities]
All staff members have responsibilities for safeguarding the company's information and computing assets
and for ensuring that third parties (such as vendors, consultants and contractors) do the same. This
involves complying with the information security policy, acceptable use policies, and all practices and
supporting procedures designed in accordance with the guidelines.
Responsibilities that are specific to a particular user should be addressed and communicated by the
company as security requirement statements. Where practicable, users should acknowledge acceptance
of responsibilities by signing a document to show statements have been received and understood.
Team members (including staff and third parties) are accountable for their actions. Any unauthorised
deviations from the information security policy may result in disciplinary action, including termination of
employment.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > User Responsibilities

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Enforcement Responsibilities
Nội dung chính: [Enforcement Responsibilities]
From time to time, in response to breaches of acceptable use policy, it may be necessary for policies to
be enforced by means of additional training or disciplinary action. This should be a function of the
company’s HR department in liaison with the Information Security Officer and the line managers of the
staff member who breached the policy.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Enforcement Responsibilities

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Training Responsibilities
Nội dung chính: [Training Responsibilities]
The ISC and Information Asset Owners are responsible for ensuring that all team members under their
responsibility receive appropriate security awareness training. Ongoing training should be carried out at
appropriate intervals or as deemed necessary. Attendance at security awareness training programs is
mandatory and should require sign off by attendees. Attendees must also confirm their understanding of
the material and their compliance with the information security policy and with any relevant acceptable
use policies.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Training Responsibilities

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 9
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 9]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 9

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
The new team member induction program should also ensure that all new team members are aware of
their information security responsibilities and the policy content as appropriate to their job function and
have signed off to show their understanding and compliance with the information security policy and with
any relevant acceptable use policies.
From time to time, it is necessary for IT staff and/or the information security officer to attend specific
technical training on security related subjects.
All staff who have contact with payment card data must receive annual awareness training about
cardholder data security. In addition, new team members must receive such training on entering a
business (or moving to a team) that handles payment card data. Records should be kept showing
successful completion of training.
All staff who use company information assets in any way should receive information security awareness
training that includes the topic of social engineering attacks. Training should include regular and frequent
reinforcement.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Independent Review Responsibilities
Nội dung chính: [Independent Review Responsibilities]
The actual practice of information security should be periodically reviewed to provide independent
assurance to senior management that company procedures and practices reflect the policy properly and
are effective.
The independent review function should, at least:
• Review the information security policy and procedures for completeness and accuracy
• Test compliance with the information security policy and procedures, as well as any external
standards to which the business must comply
• Test technical compliance and operation of systems to ensure that hardware and software
security controls have been implemented correctly (including annual penetration testing by an
independent third-party security expert of all company internet connections, internal networks,
wireless networks, and web applications).
• Report all significant security deficiencies to senior management
The independent review will be undertaken by parties with appropriate skills and experience, which may
include the internal auditor, the external auditor or independent staff from other organisations in the
company. IT management, in consultation with senior management, should be responsible for
coordinating such independent reviews.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Independent Review Responsibilities

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A1. Patching
Nội dung chính: [A1. Patching]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A1. Patching

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A1. Patching > A1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A1.1. Policy Objective]
Patching is the process of identifying, testing and deploying available patch updates to production systems
and applications to fix potential security vulnerabilities and bugs.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A1. Patching > A1.4. Policy Statements > Patch Identification
Nội dung chính: [Patch Identification]
1. Available patches should be identified and obtained from official or trusted sources. [Baseline]
2. The integrity of patches should be verified before installation by comparing hashes, either
manually or via automated digital signature verification by patch deployment tools, to ensure that
the patch obtained is correct and unaltered. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A1. Patching > A1.4. Policy Statements > Patch Identification

Nội dung chính: [Patch Testing]
1. Patches must be tested in a testing environment prior to production implementation to ensure
that the patch deployment would not lead to production impacts or degradation of security
posture. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. Routine patch updates should be deployed at least quarterly. [Baseline]
3. A patch management schedule should be maintained, detailing the following as a minimum:
[Baseline]
o patch name;
o patch deployment date;
o all systems/applications in scope;
o System Owner; and
o system criticality/priority e.g., Highly Critical/Highly Critical/Critical/Non-critical.
4. A rollback strategy should be established before the patch is applied. [Baseline]
5. Only trained administrators who have appropriate authorization are responsible for deploying
patches. The patch deployment process should follow A6. IT Change Control. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Patch Management
Nội dung chính: [Patch Management]
1. A patch management tool should be put in place to identify and prioritize patch updates,
customize scheduling of patch implementation and reporting of patch compliance status.
[Baseline]
2. Automatic patch updates for production systems and applications must be disabled on systems
where there is a risk of disruption or unavailability. [Baseline]
3. Exceptions to the security patch deployment with corresponding compensating controls should
be reviewed and approved by the company’s Cyber Security Team at least quarterly. If the
company does not have a dedicated Cyber Security Team, the review should be done by an IT
member with cyber security role and approved by the Head of IT. [Baseline]
4. A monitoring mechanism (e.g., regular patch management reports) should be established to
ensure that security patches are tested and implemented within the patch deployment
timeframes prescribed above. [Advanced]
5. Virtual patching should be implemented to reduce risk exposure when no security patches are
available. [Advanced]
6. Patch management reports should be reviewed to ensure that security patches are tested and
implemented within the patch implementation schedule. [Advanced]

Nội dung chính: [Configuration Standard]
1. Configuration standards for all systems and network components should be developed with
reference to the guidelines published by reputable sources and vendor recommended security
controls, such as Center for Internet Security and Microsoft. [Baseline]
2. Configuration standards should be reviewed by the Cyber Security Team at least annually.
[Baseline]

2. All exceptions to configuration standards, with justifications, should be reviewed and approved
by the Cyber Security Team at least annually. [Baseline]
3. Time setting of all hosts should follow the Technical Annex - Time Synchronisation. [Baseline]
4. Configuration management tool should be used for detection of unauthorized configuration
changes and automation of configuration baselining and hardening. [Advanced]
5. All non-console administrative access is encrypted using strong cryptography. [PCI] [Advanced]
6. A golden image should be used for consistency. It should be created and maintained in accordance
with the established configuration standards, and ideally, there will be a different golden image
for different tiers of application/security risk (e.g. DMZ-hosted, client workstations, internal
server). [Advanced]
7. Configuration standards and golden images should be tested using vulnerability scanners or
penetration testing to ensure they are secure before deployment in production. [Baseline]

content:

Nội dung chính: [A3.1. Policy Objective]
Information systems must be regularly checked against an updated list of security vulnerabilities and to
detect any configuration weaknesses so as to reduce the risk of a system compromise.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A3. Vulnerability Management > A3.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A3. Vulnerability Management > A3.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [A3.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of the company’s network infrastructure, operating systems and
applications developed, maintained, operated or owned by the company, including those hosted in
external environments (e.g., public cloud).
For 3rd party assets and applications, please refer to the F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A3. Vulnerability Management > A3.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A3. Vulnerability Management > A3.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [A3.3. Definition]
Different types of vulnerability scans are listed as follows:
o Internal Scan – Credentialed scanning of infrastructure within the company’s trusted network or
any hosted infrastructure capable of scanning registry files, operating systems, applications or
device firmware within all of the company’s environments;
o External Scan – Both whitelisted and non-whitelisted scanning on the perimeter of the company’s
network or any externally available hosted infrastructure;
o Application Scan – Scanning of the company’s web and mobile applications with a focus on
OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities, 3rd party software components and common environment or
deployment issues;
o Source Code Scan – Scanning of the company’s in-house developed application’s source code to
ensure secure coding practices such as OWASP Secure Coding Practices has been followed and
applications are free from coding weaknesses, including in imported 3rd party code;
o Cloud Configuration Scan – Scanning of cloud services’ configuration such as data encryption,
password policies, access controls, backup and restoration settings, data exposure and privilege
escalation etc.; and
o Container Security Scan – Scanning of 3rd party containers used to build and run applications to
ensure they do not contain vulnerabilities in code or configuration.
Different types of penetration tests are listed as follows:
o Network-layer Penetration Test – Real-world attack simulation against the company’s network
infrastructure; and
o Application-layer Penetration Test – Real-world attack simulation against the company’s system
focusing on application related vulnerabilities such as injection flaws, cross-site scripting, broken

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A3.4. Policy Statements > Vulnerability Detection
Nội dung chính: [Vulnerability Detection]
1. The Cyber Security Team or IT Manager is responsible for ensuring that vulnerabilities are
vulnerabilities are identified from vulnerability scan, penetration test, and multiple threat
intelligence feeds as per the requirements listed within A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence. [Baseline]
2. External vulnerability scan should be performed at least weekly and internal vulnerability scan
should be performed at least monthly, and before the production launch of any new applications,
infrastructure equipment or major system changes. [Baseline]
3. Internal vulnerability scans should include authenticated scanning as follows: [Advanced] [PCI]
• Systems that are unable to accept credentials for authenticated scanning are documented.
• Sufficient privileges are used for those systems that accept credentials for scanning.
• If accounts used for authenticated scanning can be used for interactive login, they are
managed in accordance with the requirements specified in D1. Logical Access
Management.
4. Vulnerability scanning should be performed using settings that would not disrupt the normal
usage of systems. Notifications must be provided to affected System Owner prior to the scan such
that all targets can be scanned while minimizing any possible business disruptions. [Baseline]
o Host-based vulnerability scanning agent should be installed on all workstations to ensure that
remote machines can be scanned. [Advanced]
5. Penetration test should be performed at least yearly, before the production launch of any major
system changes to identify vulnerabilities within the systems. [Baseline]
6. Penetration testing should cover, at a minimum, all external facing systems, applications and
infrastructure. [Baseline]
7. Security and configuration scanner should be used to scan cloud environments at least monthly
so as to identify potential misconfigurations that might lead to security breaches. [Baseline]
8. Red team exercise should be performed at least annually to test the effectiveness of the security
controls related to detection and prevention of cyber-attacks. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A3.4. Policy Statements > Vulnerability Detection

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A3.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 18

9. External vulnerability scans on the cardholder data environment must be performed by a Payment
Card Industry Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV). [PCI]
10. A Web Application Firewall (WAF) or equivalent automated technical solution should be deployed
for public-facing web applications to continually detect and prevent web-based attacks, with at
least the following: [PCI] [Advanced]
• Is installed in front of public-facing web applications and is configured to detect and prevent
web-based attacks.
• Actively running and up to date as applicable.
• Generating audit logs.
• Configured to either block web-based attacks or generate an alert that is immediately
investigated.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Improvement Informational Informational Informational
Nội dung chính: [Improvement Informational Informational Informational]
3. “Critical”, “High” and “Medium” risk items must be fixed whilst “Low” and “Improvement” risk
items are subjected to business and its risk acceptance. [Baseline]
4. Re-scans must be performed to validate vulnerabilities are fixed after patch application. [Baseline]
5. Risk exemptions can only be granted if the remediation is not feasible or if there are potential
adverse impact to the company’s assets and operations. Granting of exemptions and the
compensating controls such as disabling the service, tightening the access control etc. must be
reviewed and approved and their implementations verified by the company’s Cyber Security
Team. [Baseline]
6. All risk exemptions must be documented and reviewed at least quarterly as part of the risk
management process to ensure that there is no change regarding the imposed risk. If there is an
increased risk, additional compensating controls or remedial actions must be performed to reduce
the acceptable risk level. [Baseline]
7. Remedial actions for vulnerabilities driven by critical security incidents should follow the “Urgent
change” process as referenced to A6. IT Change Control. Other remedial actions should be
referenced to the normal change process. If patching is involved, the patch management process
including patch analysis, testing and deployment must be referenced to A1. Patching. [Baseline]
8. The information obtained from the vulnerability management process could be shared with
appropriate or authorised personnel from within the Group only (i.e. within the Cybersecurity
Special Interest Group). [Baseline]
9. A centralized vulnerability management platform integrated with various vulnerability scanning
tools should be deployed to prioritize and keep track of remediation items for confirmed
vulnerabilities related to applications, systems and infrastructure. [Advanced]
10. Quarterly external vulnerability scans performed by ASV cannot be deemed as a passing scan until
there are no vulnerabilities rated 4.0 or higher by the CVSS and no automatic failures. For internal
scans, all “High” and “Critical” vulnerabilities must be fixed. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A4.1. Policy Objective]
To protect company systems against intrusion by malware, leading to the compromise of systems or data.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [A4.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all company systems. The anti-malware controls for remote connection
should follow the D3. Remote Access & Client VPN.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [A4.3. Definition]
Anti-malware includes Endpoint Protection Platform, Endpoint Detection and Response, Secure Email
Gateway, IDS/IPS and Web Proxy URL Filtering. For Endpoint Detection and Response and IDS/IPS please
refer to the H1. Network Security.
Anti-virus software scans an operating system or a file system for known malware such as trojans, worms,
and ransomware, and upon detecting them, removes them from the system.
Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) is a solution that collects data from endpoints, and provides
advanced measures for detecting threats, with the ability to identify where an attack originated from and
how it is spreading. The company should understand the features offered by its EDR solution as it may
include traditional anti-virus features, thereby eliminating the need to purchase anti-virus software.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A4.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.4. Policy Statements > Installation of anti-malware software
Nội dung chính: [Installation of anti-malware software]
1. Secure email gateway and web proxy must be implemented to scan emails, including email
attachment and URL links, and web traffics for malicious content. [Baseline]
2. Real-time anti-malware tools must be installed from trusted or formal sources. More details for
anti-malware tools on mobile device can be found within J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management.
[Baseline]
3. Anti-malware software, specifically Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solution, must be
installed on all company servers, desktops and laptops to scan data both on arriving and leaving
the company system in real time. The installation should be done before systems are deployed
on production networks. The anti-malware software must also be able to carry out scans of the
computer’s internal drives weekly. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.4. Policy Statements > Installation of anti-malware software

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 22
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 22]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A4. Anti-Malware > A4.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 22

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
4. Network-based virus-scanners should not be provided by the same vendor as the workstation and
server anti-virus tools to provide additional protection. [Advanced]
5. Anti-malware mechanisms cannot be disabled or altered by users, unless specifically documented,
and authorized by management on a case-by-case basis for a limited time period. A follow-up
process must be implemented to restore normal operation as soon as practical and an alerting
mechanism used to generate reminders every 24 hours after the expiry of the time period
authorised. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Configuration
Nội dung chính: [Configuration]
1. The anti-malware software must be configured to scan removable devices / media for viruses
upon connecting the host. [Baseline]
2. Real-time anti-malware solutions must be configured to send alerts to the relevant IT Team and
Cyber Security Team in real time when they detect any malwares or suspicious / malicious
behaviour, and the handling procedure must be defined following the I1. Incident Response.
[Baseline]
3. Virus signatures, malicious code definitions as well as their detection and repair engines must be
downloaded on a daily basis, or as frequently as the tools support, and these updates are
distributed to all protected devices within the same day. All downloads and distribution activities
must be automated. [Baseline]
4. All anti-malware’s alert and activity logs must be configured to log to a central location so that
incidents and events can be tracked. Logs should include information about any malware detected
(and how that was resolved), as well as notifications about signature and engine updates following
the A5. System Logging and Monitoring. [Baseline]
5. Opening an e-mail attachment or URL are automatically isolated in a secure container or virtual
environment so that malware can be analysed but cannot access vital data, end-point operating
systems, or applications on the network [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Configuration

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Operations
Nội dung chính: [Operations]
1. IT Team should review the anti-malware console (or its report) at least once every 2 weeks to
check the endpoint protection status and ensure connected systems have received updates within
the last 48 hours. [Baseline]
2. IT Team should follow up on the endpoints which are shown as offline, and its signature is
outdated for more than 7 days. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Operations

3. IT Team should scan any external removable devices / media for any viruses before connecting
them to the company network or systems. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Handling outbreaks of malware
Nội dung chính: [Handling outbreaks of malware]
1. A formal procedure for actions to be taken in the event of a malware related outbreak should be
created, including defined roles and responsibilities. The containment, response and recovery
process must follow I1. Incident Response. [Baseline]
2. All staff must be trained, as part of overall security awareness training, to report any actual or
suspected outbreaks of malware. IT Team must respond to confirmed malware attacks in
accordance with the I1. Incident Response. [Baseline]
3. When a malware outbreak occurs and the anti-malware fails to detect the malware, IT Team
should request the anti-malware vendor for an updated detection process or signature. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A5.1. Policy Objective]
To develop a guideline that clearly define mandatory requirements and suggested recommendations for
log management activities.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [A5.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all company production system and network components.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [A5.3. Definition]
Event logs are used to record user activities, exceptions, faults, and information security events.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A5.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.4. Policy Statements > Event logging
Nội dung chính: [Event logging]
1. The correct setting of computer clocks following Technical Guideline -Time Synchronisation must
be implemented to ensure the accuracy of audit logs. [Baseline]
2. Event logs must not contain personal data, passwords or PINs. [Baseline]
3. The following events should be logged: [Baseline]
o All individual user accesses to cardholder data
o All actions taken by any individual with root or administrative privileges
o Access to all audit trails
o Invalid logical access attempts
o Use of and changes to identification and authentication mechanisms—including but not
limited to creation of new accounts and elevation of privileges—and all changes,
additions, or deletions to accounts with root or administrative privileges
o Initialization, stopping, or pausing of the audit logs
o Creation and deletion of system-level objects
4. Event logs should contain enough details to link the events to an individual person or system
account. They should also maintain details necessary to perform a forensic investigation in the
event of a breach or compromise. This detail should include the following if available and
applicable: [Baseline]
o user identification;
o machine identification;

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.4. Policy Statements > Event logging

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 25
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 25]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A5. System Logging and Monitoring > A5.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 25

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
o date and time stamp;
o name of component, database object, or resource originating the event;
o name of component, database object, or resource affected by the event;
o description of the activity performed;
o event ID or event type (e.g., access failure);
o referring page (in case of HTTP access);
o changes to system configuration;
o use of system utilities and applications;
o network addresses and protocols;
o alarms raised by the access control system;
o activation and de-activation of protection systems, such as anti-virus systems and
intrusion detection systems;
o records of transactions executed by users in applications;
o other information or detail that may help to recreate a sequence of events to provide
information for debugging or testing purposes;
o escalation of privileges (e.g., sudo).
5. To help operating companies decide which logs to capture, the following list of log sources can be
used as a guide: [Advanced]
• Security software
o Anti-malware / endpoint protection software
o Intruder detection and intruder prevention tools
o Remote access software
o Web proxies and SSL/TLS web traffic inspection proxies
o Vulnerability management software
o Authentication servers
o Routers and switches, and any other network infrastructure devices (e.g. load
balancers and wireless access points/controllers)
o Firewalls and other security appliances
o Automated code review tools (if used)
• Operating systems - workstations, servers and appliances
o System events - start-up, shut-down, and services starting and stopping
o Audit records - authentication (success or fail), user accounts added/deleted,
passwords changed (success or fail), any changes to privileges
• Applications
o Client requests and server responses, not just in the context of web server logs but
accesses and attempts to access data in any context
o Account information, creation of accounts, authentication (failed and successful), use
of privileged accounts
o Usage information, relating to the quantity or volume of requests, to identify
anomalies
o Major actions, like the application being started, re-started or stopped, inability to
access a database, or other significant failures

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Protection and Retention of Event Logs
Nội dung chính: [Protection and Retention of Event Logs]
1. Security events must be logged and monitored in a centralized system. [Baseline]
2. Event logs must be retained for at least six months. [Baseline]
3. System logs used as forensic evidence must be retained following the I1. Incident Response.
[Baseline]
4. Logs must be classified as “Confidential” information or higher. [Baseline]
5. Logs must be protected from unauthorized access, modification and deletion. [Baseline]
6. Access to logs must be restricted. Ability to delete audit logs shall be restricted to only privilege
IDs (e.g. root or administrator). The privileged user accounts for accessing and manipulating logs
must be managed by the D1. Logical Access Management. [Baseline]
7. Event logs must be backed up and the log transmission and transportation requirement must
follow the A8. Data Backup. [Baseline]
8. Real-time copying of logs must be forwarded to log servers in order to protect the log integrity.
[Advanced]
9. Relevant audit log history of critical systems such as systems components processing highly
confidential data (including credit card data), systems critical for operations, and servers and
systems that perform security functions (such as authentication), must be retained for at least
one year with a minimum of three months immediately available for analysis. (e.g. online,
archived, or restorable from backup). [Baseline] [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Protection and Retention of Event Logs

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Monitoring and Analysis of Logs
Nội dung chính: [Monitoring and Analysis of Logs]
1. Monitoring activities such as ones identified below should be put in place to identify and analyse
anomalous behaviour:[Baseline]
a. review successful and unsuccessful attempts to access protected resources [e.g. domain
name system (DNS) servers, web portals and file shares];
b. check DNS logs to identify outbound network connections to malicious servers, such as those
associated with botnet command and control servers;

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Monitoring and Analysis of Logs

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 27
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 27]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 27

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
c. examine usage reports from service providers (e.g. invoices or service reports) for unusual
activity within systems and networks (e.g. by reviewing patterns of activity);
d. include event logs of physical monitoring such as entrance and exit to ensure more accurate
detection and incident analysis;
e. correlate logs to enable efficient and highly accurate analysis.
2. Security events must be forwarded to SIEM system with correlation capability for centralized
logging and real-time monitoring. Monitoring of log outages should be performed to ensure
completeness of logs monitored. [Baseline]
3. The correction rules of SIEM should be reviewed and updated at least annually or upon a
significant infrastructural change. [Advanced]
4. Analysis of logs should be performed with consideration of the following to interpret information
security events which can represent indicators of compromise:
a. having the necessary skills for the experts performing the analysis;
b. following managed security service provider (MSSP) procedures for log analysis
c. identifying the required attributes of each security-related event;
d. exceptions identified through the use of predetermined rules [e.g. security information and
event management (SIEM) or firewall rules, and intrusion detection systems (IDSs) or malware
signatures];
e. known behaviour patterns and standard network traffic compared to anomalous activity and
behaviour [user and entity behaviour analytics (UEBA)];
f. results of trend or pattern analysis (e.g. as a result of using data analytics, big data techniques
and specialized analysis tools);
g. available threat intelligence.
5. Threat hunting solutions with deep-learning capability should be deployed on the infrastructure
to gather and analyse logs in order to detect zero-day threats. [Advanced]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control
Nội dung chính: [A6. IT Change Control]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A6.1. Policy Objective]
Building proper change control procedures can ensure that proposed changes will not disrupt systems,
users, and services and reduce unauthorized or improper changes that could severely impact critical
applications of the corporate.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [A6.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all company systems and infrastructure components.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [A6.3. Definition]
Normal changes are changes that are categorized, prioritized, planned, and that follows all approvals
before deployment. Emergency changes are requests for change to support urgent implementation where
there is an inherent risk in delay. These changes are treated as exceptions to the normal change
management process.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A6.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.4. Policy Statements > Normal Change Control
Nội dung chính: [Normal Change Control]
1. Routine change control procedures must be established to include the recording of all changes,
planning and testing of changes, assessment of the method of implementation and the necessity
of changes, a formal approval procedure for changes, communication of change details to all
relevant parties, and procedures for aborting and recovering from unsuccessful changes.
[Baseline]
2. Production and development systems must be separated either physically or logically. Production
migration procedures should be defined, and segregation of duties should be enforced. When a
system is moved into production, all non-essential files relating to its development must be
removed from the production version. [Baseline]
3. Secure software lifecycle procedures should be used to ensure that all amendments must be
tested in testing environment before deployment in production. Where this is not possible due to
technical constraints, other change control procedures should be used to ensure an audit trail,
suitable approval and sufficient testing are in place for change before implementation. All changes
should be fully tested and documented. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.4. Policy Statements > Normal Change Control

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 29
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 29]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A6. IT Change Control > A6.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 29

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
4. To prevent unauthorized changes to web sites, especially on payment pages, a change- and
tamper-detection mechanism should be deployed as follows: [PCI][Advanced]
• To alert personnel to unauthorized modification (including indicators of compromise,
changes, additions, and deletions) to the HTTP headers and the contents of payment
pages as received by the consumer browser.
• The mechanism is configured to evaluate the received HTTP header and payment page.
• The mechanism functions are performed as follows:
o At least once every seven days

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > OR

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
8. A technical review of business-critical applications should be conducted after operating platform
changes. Operating platforms include operating systems, databases and middleware platforms.
This process should cover: [Advanced]
o review of application controls and integrity procedures;
o ensuring that notification of operating platform changes is provided in time to allow
appropriate tests and reviews to take place before implementation; and
o ensuring that appropriate changes are made to relevant business continuity plans.
9. The change management system uses thresholds to determine when a risk assessment of the
impact of the change is required. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Emergency Change Control
Nội dung chính: [Emergency Change Control]
1. Emergency changes must be approved by the IT Senior Management (e.g. Head of IT, Head of
Application) and System Owner via internal communication channels (e.g., email) before applying
the changes. [Baseline]
2. Emergency changes must be recorded following the defined change procedure, and where
technically feasible an automated audit trail of the emergency change implementation must be
generated, logging all relevant activities. The implementer making the emergency change must
be required to provide a written description of what was done to address the emergency. The
initial cause of the emergency must also be documented. [Baseline]
3. Post-implementation review must be performed by manager of the team that implemented
changes to verify that the emergency changes have been properly implemented and all required
documents have been properly recorded. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A7.1. Policy Objective]
Capacity planning and performance monitoring should be in place to ensure that all current and future
capacity and performance aspects of IT infrastructure and systems are provided to meet business
requirements at an acceptable cost.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.1. Policy Objective

Nội dung chính: [A7.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all IT systems that support business operations, except for the use of SaaS
on public cloud.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A7.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.4. Policy Statements > Capacity planning
Nội dung chính: [Capacity planning]
1. Business Owners have the responsibility to obtain funding in order to support their IT capacity
infrastructure needs. This includes initial procurement as well as maintaining needs. [Baseline]
2. A thorough review of the system capacity of high priority systems and infrastructure should be
conducted at least annually to identify any potential weaknesses that may affect the stability and
performance of business operations. [Baseline]
3. System Owners and IT Team should identify and document capacity requirements by considering
how critical the system is to the business. [Baseline]
4. System Owners and IT Team should define the thresholds of performance data for their systems.
The performance data includes but not limited to memory usage, processor utilization, storage or
disk space, network statistics, bandwidth utilization, service response time. If a system reaches
the threshold, IT Team/administrator shall take corrective actions to resolve the performance
issue. [Baseline]
5. For business-critical system, capacity plan should be produced quarterly and performance reports,
monthly. Otherwise, annual capacity planning and performance reports should be produced for

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.4. Policy Statements > Capacity planning

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 32

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A7. Capacity Planning > A7.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 32

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
related Business Owner, System Owner and other stakeholders, such as senior management.
[Baseline]
6. Any necessary enhancement measures to rectify the identified weaknesses should also be
implemented promptly to avoid possible system instability. [Baseline]
7. System utilization threshold and the corresponding precautionary measures should be defined.
[Baseline]
8. Methodology should be developed to help forecast of resource utilization and performance
requirement and turn business requirements into IT capacity plans. [Baseline]
9. For critical business systems, prior arrangement with the related software and hardware
providers to allow upgrading of system capacity within a short period of time when such a need
arises. [Advanced]
10. End-to-end stress testing should be conducted with adequate coverage of all relevant systems
and infrastructure components. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Performance monitoring
Nội dung chính: [Performance monitoring]
1. An automated performance monitoring and alert system that monitors all critical systems and
infrastructure components supporting the high priority business operations both during and after
office hours, should be in place. [Baseline]
2. Designated staff should handle any potential system interruption or performance degradation
detected both during and after office hours in a timely manner. [Baseline]
3. If system tuning is needed, IT Team/administrator should tune the system by initiating a change
request in accordance with A6. IT Change Control. [Baseline]
4. Notification mechanism should be in place to report any alerts received and fixed to System
Owner and related stakeholder to review and discuss long term solution e.g., purchase additional
hardware, license. [Baseline]

Nội dung chính: [A8.1. Policy Objective]
Adequate backup can protect against data loss and reduce impact from a disaster, media and
system failures.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.1. Policy Objective

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [A8.3. Definition]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A8.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.4. Policy Statements > Frequency of backup
Nội dung chính: [Frequency of backup]
1. The extent (e.g. full or differential backup) and frequency of backups and level of storage security
must depend on: [Baseline]
o the value of the data to the business;
o the cost-effectiveness of the backup solutions;
o any limitations of the applications or technologies involved.
2. All backup schedules must be aligned to the business continuity plans, regulatory and the
Information Retention Policy and Guideline. [Baseline]
3. Execution of backups must be checked daily to ensure they have completed successfully, and all
issues should be followed up on a daily basis or next working day. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.4. Policy Statements > Frequency of backup

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.4. Policy Statements > Information backup
Nội dung chính: [Information backup]
1. Logs must be kept of all backups taken including their success or failure and regular or consistent
backup failures must be assessed, reasons for failure analysed and appropriate fixes put in place
to resolve the issue. [Baseline]
2. All relevant data must be backed up to ensure full and consistent system recoveries are achievable
where required. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A8. Data Backup > A8.4. Policy Statements > Information backup

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
3. Restore drill must be performed annually covering core business systems and IT services to ensure
data can be restored form backup. Where appropriate the restorations must take into account
different scenarios including: [Baseline]
o application failure, e.g. rolling back to previous day;
o media failure, e.g. re-building a failed disk on a server;
o full restoration of a server, e.g. after major equipment failure or for disaster recovery
requirements.
4. Any backup material disposed of must be destroyed appropriately following the C2. Asset Disposal.
[Baseline]
5. The system user activities (e.g., modifying backup data and removing the backup data) of
operational staff must be recorded and maintained as an activity log (including the date and time
of activity, description, any errors, verification and name of staff). The log must be review
following the A5. System Logging and Monitoring. [Baseline]
6. Confidential backup data must be encrypted by separate encryption key following the K1.
Cryptography. The encryption key must be kept by the System Owner. [Baseline]
7. Backup servers must not be authenticated by AD and the backup job must not be performed by
using a domain administrator account. [Baseline]
8. Three copies of data including one production copy and two backup copies must be kept on two
different media (e.g. disk and tape) with one copy in off-site (can be cloud platform). [Baseline]
9. All backups should have offline, isolated or immutable copies to ensure that intruders cannot
destroy them prior to launching a ransomware attack. [Baseline]
10. Backup equipment (e.g., backup servers and tape library) should be in place in the DR site or
provided by the service provider for data recovery. [Baseline]
11. Dedicated backup jobs/ tapes should be used for confidential data backup to ensure the
confidential data is held separately from other data. [Advanced]
12. IT Team should define the data recovery procedure for handling the loss of encryption key for
confidential data backup. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Backup location
Nội dung chính: [Backup location]
1. Backup media held on and off site must be stored in fireproof storage areas or other suitable
locations with appropriate environmental conditions, e.g. temperature kept within media
tolerance levels. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Backup location

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 35
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 35]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 35

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. Backup media held off-site must be kept secure from unauthorised access but must be easily and
quickly recoverable in the event of an emergency and should not rely on one member of staff for
its recovery (e.g. storage at a member of staff's home is not acceptable). Alternate key holders or
staff authorised to request media be returned to site must be available. [Baseline]
3. The off-site location for backup media should be far enough away so that it is unlikely to be
affected by a major disaster affecting the site being backed up - as a minimum at least 800 metres
away and in a different building. The location should be at least as secure as the location from
which the data was taken. Security of the backup storage location should be reviewed annually.
[PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Backup media
Nội dung chính: [Backup media]
1. Backup media must meet all requirements of the backup device vendor, and must be replaced, or
taken out of the usage cycle on a regular basis and in line with the media vendor's
recommendations. [Baseline]
2. The backup tape inventory must be updated every 6 months to ensure no backup tapes are missed.
[Baseline]
3. All backup media transported offsite or stored outside of a company managed facility must be
encrypted/password protected and approved by IT Operation Manager. A secure courier or
transportation services with secure packaging must be used for backup media transportation.
[Baseline]
4. All backup media must be labelled appropriately, and its location recorded, tracked and audited
semi-annually. [Baseline]
5. The cloud platform can be used to store backup data. If cloud is used for data backup,
requirements in section J2. Cloud Computing must be met. [Baseline]
6. When the backup tapes are broken, the backup tapes should be sent to the trusted vendor to
repair the tapes following the 3rd Party / Vendor Management. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Backup media

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Cloud Backup
Nội dung chính: [Cloud Backup]
1. Backup services provided in the cloud do not exactly fit the definitions and requirements listed
above. The IT Team should conduct an assessment to ensure the cloud backup can meet the
resilience and performance objectives of the business and satisfy the goals of the requirements
listed above. The assessment should cover: [Baseline]
o Ability to meet defined business needs (RTO, RPO);

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Cloud Backup

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 36

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 36

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
o Ability to meet performance objectives for backup and restoration, including testing
thereof;
o Ability to meet confidentiality, integrity and availability requirements such as through
data encryption, immutability of storage, geographical distance from production cloud
data centres, etc.
2. Vendors of SaaS applications and PaaS infrastructure may not be able to meet traditional backup
objectives such as being able to restore to a specific point in time. The IT Team should conduct a
risk assessment to ensure the data protection capabilities provided can meet the business’
requirements as part of the selection process of any SaaS applications or PaaS platforms. 3rd party
backup tools may be used if they can satisfy shortcomings in native capabilities provided by the
vendor. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention
Nội dung chính: [A9. Information Retention]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A9.1. Policy Objective]
Information retention is to define a recognized proven procedure for managing company information. It
ensures company systems remain available to those who need them and mitigate the legal and
operational risks associated with inconsistently controlled information, and data privacy issues.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [A9.2. Scope]
This guideline is applicable to all of the company’s information including customer personally identifiable
information (PII) data, financial transactions and intellectual property (IP) if any.
