content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A9.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.4. Policy Statements > Retention Period Definition
Nội dung chính: [Retention Period Definition]
1. Information Owners are responsible for specifying and documenting the information retention
period. Retention must take into account the following: [Baseline]
o Company, tax and other statutory or legal requirements;
o Operational requirements;
o Information classification level.
2. Annual review on the information retention periods must be conducted by Information Owner.
[Baseline]
3. Depending on the legal compliance requirements, e-mail – inbound, outbound and including
attachments – must be retained for a defined period of time in accordance with regulatory and
business requirements. That period may be defined by the relevant law (to be determined in
accordance with legal advisors). If not, the period should be no less than one month, and should
be longer if storage and processing capabilities permit. [Baseline]
4. A retention policy for different types of personal information must be established. [Baseline]
5. All audit trails must be retained for a period of 12 months, or according to legislative, regulatory
or audit requirements. [Baseline]
6. Sensitive authentication data from payment cards (e.g. the card validation code) must not be
retained at all after authorization. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.4. Policy Statements > Retention Period Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 38
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 38]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A9. Information Retention > A9.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 38

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
7. Audit trails for tracking and monitoring all access to network resources and cardholder data
environment must be retained for at least one year, with a minimum of three months immediately
available for analysis. [PCI]
8. Personal information should be retained following relevant legal requirement, including:
o Recruitment-related data of a job applicant should not be retained for more than 2 years
from the date of rejecting the applicant. [PDPO]
o Employment-related data of an employee should not be retained for more than 7 years from
the date the employee leaves employment. [PDPO]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Implementation
Nội dung chính: [Implementation]
1. System Owners are responsible for retaining information during the retention periods set by the
Information Owners and deleting or destroying information after the retention periods. An
annual check must be conducted to ensure that expired data are securely deleted or destroyed.
[Baseline]
2. Information retained that exceeds the retention period must be approved by Data Protection
Officer or equivalent roles. [Baseline]
3. Information that need not be retained must be disposed of in accordance with associated disposal
guidelines. [Baseline]
4. Conduct annual review (e.g. by checklist review) to verify the compliance with data handling
requirements in documented policies and guidelines. [Baseline]
5. The company should fully understand what data privacy regulations (local and international) are
applicable to the PII it holds (both customer & employee). Relevant policies and procedures
should be developed accordingly. All staff should be trained at least annually regarding the
handling of PII and relevant requirements such as regulatory notification to promote and ensure
compliance with the regulations. [Baseline]
6. Conduct regular scanning of applications and file servers at least quarterly to determine whether
there are undocumented or unauthorised storage of PII. [Advanced]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A10.1. Policy Objective]
Cyber Threat Intelligence should be leveraged to enrich the company’s defensive capabilities and
vulnerability management process.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [A10.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of the company’s IT asset.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [A10.3. Definition]
Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) refers to information which can help the company to identify, assess,
monitor, and respond to cyber-threats. CTI helps with identifying and analysing the methods, capabilities
and technologies that may have been used by hackers. Such information can help the company to
anticipate potential security breaches before they occur and to respond quickly to confirmed incidents.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A10.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.4. Policy Statements > Cyber Threat Intelligence Monitoring
Nội dung chính: [Cyber Threat Intelligence Monitoring]
1. A CTI monitoring process must be implemented by the company to collect relevant threat
intelligence from multiple feeds and sources, including both external and internal. This activity
should include identifying, vetting and selecting internal and external information sources that
are necessary and appropriate to provide information required for the production of threat
intelligence. [Baseline]
2. The Cyber Security Team should analyse the CTI to evaluate the cyber threat in terms of cyber risk
and specific actions required to mitigate the risk. Such information should be viewed within the
context of the company’s risk profile and risk appetite to prioritise mitigating actions in
anticipation of threats. [Baseline]
3. The Cyber Security Team should review the CTI monitoring process at least yearly to ensure that
such process is producing accurate and updated information. [Baseline]
4. CTI should be integrated to the company’s security information and event management system
(SIEM) and other security products like EDR and firewall to prioritize the security alerts and
proactively block attacks. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A10. Cyber Threat Intelligence > A10.4. Policy Statements > Cyber Threat Intelligence Monitoring

areas such as staff behaviour, security defences, policies and resources. [Advanced]

content:

Nội dung chính: [Vulnerability Management]
1. In case the CTI indicated that there are potential threats or vulnerabilities relevant to the
company’s network infrastructure, operating systems or applications, the Cyber Security Team
should identify the affected systems and perform the remedial actions. The remediation timeline
and process should be referenced to the A3. Vulnerability Management. [Baseline]

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A11. Information Security in Project Management

content:

Information security should be addressed in project management, regardless of the type of the project.

Nội dung chính: [A11.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of IT projects.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A11. Information Security in Project Management > A11.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A11.4. Policy Statements]
1. The project management methods in use should require that: [Baseline]
o information security objectives are included in project objectives;
o an information security risk assessment is conducted at an early stage of the project to
identify necessary controls; and
o information security is part of all phases of the applied project methodology.
2. Information security implications should be addressed and reviewed regularly in all projects.
[Baseline]
3. Responsibilities for information security should be defined and allocated to specified roles defined in
the project management methods. [Baseline]

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment
Nội dung chính: [A12. Risk Assessment]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [A12.1. Policy Objective]
Perform periodic information security risk assessments for the purpose of determining areas of
vulnerability, and to initiate appropriate remediation.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [A12.2. Scope]
Risk Assessments can be conducted on any information system, to include applications, servers, and
networks, and any process or procedure by which these systems are administered and/or maintained.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.3. Definition > N/A
Nội dung chính: [N/A]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.3. Definition > N/A

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [A12.4. Policy Statements]
1. The execution, development and implementation of remediation programs should be the joint
responsibility of business' security department and the department responsible for the system area
being assessed. Employees should cooperate fully with any risk assessment being conducted on
systems for which they are held accountable and work with the business' security department in the
development of a remediation plan. For additional information, refer to Technology Risk Procedure
documentation. [Baseline]
2. A targeted risk analysis should be performed for each PCI DSS requirement that the entity meets
with a customized approach. The analysis must be performed at least once every 12 months. [PCI]
3. PCI DSS scope should be documented and confirmed at least annually. Each PCI DSS requirement
that provides flexibility for how frequently it is performed (for example, requirements to be
performed periodically) is supported by a targeted risk analysis that is documented and includes:
[PCI]
• Identification of the assets being protected.
• Identification of the threat(s) that the requirement is protecting against.
• Identification of factors that contribute to the likelihood and/or impact of a threat being
realized.
• Resulting analysis that determines, and includes justification for, how frequently the
requirement must be performed to minimize the likelihood of the threat being realized.
• Review of each targeted risk analysis at least once every 12 months to determine whether
the results are still valid or if an updated risk analysis is needed.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 44
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 44]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > A12. Risk Assessment > A12.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 44

4. Performance of updated risk analyses when needed, as determined by the annual review. Any
exception to the policy must be formally risk accepted by the requesting department head and
approved by the business' ISC in advance. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security
Nội dung chính: [B1. Physical Security]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [B1.1. Policy Objective]
Physical access controls can prevent unauthorized access, data leakage, damage and interruption to
business activities.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [B1.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to the company’s staff and office premises, along with vendors, visitors and
other 3rd party personnel. For logical access control, please refer to the D1. Logical Access Management.
For physical security of outsourced areas such as outsourced data centres, their physical security controls
should be assessed during the third-party risk assessment as referenced to the F1. 3rd Party / Vendor
Management.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [B1.3. Definition]
Apart from normal office premises, sensitive areas include places such as data centres, computer control
room or other areas housing IT facilities supporting critical business activities, including, but not limited
to, servers, firewalls, switches, routers, IP telephony systems etc.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [B1.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.4. Policy Statements > Physical Access Control
Nội dung chính: [Physical Access Control]
1. Physical access rights of the company’s staff must be assigned on a need-to-have basis. A list of
physical access rights for each individual job role should be maintained, kept up-to-date and
reviewed at least yearly. [Baseline]
2. Entry by visitors such as vendor support staff, maintenance staff, project teams or other external
parties must not be allowed unless accompanied by authorised staff such as security guards,
receptionists or other staff. Visitors should be issued with temporary permits and wear
identification badges at all times, allowing the staff to identify them easily. [Baseline]
3. All staff must be encouraged to ensure the security of their offices. They should challenge or
report any individual within office premises without a staff ID or visitor permit. Any suspicious
personnel and activities such as piggybacking should also be reported. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.4. Policy Statements > Physical Access Control

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 47
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 47]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B1. Physical Security > B1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 47

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
4. An access list must be used to record all visitors’ access and the temporary permits distributed to
them. The list must be retained for at least three months and reviewed at least monthly. [Baseline]
5. A visitor log including the following information should be maintained to keep track of the visitors:
[Baseline]
o Visitor’s identity (as documented on the national identity card or driving license etc.);
o Visitor’s organization;
o Purpose for the visit;
o Access location;
o Visitor badge / temporary permit’s identification number;
o Date and time of entry and departure.
6. Access to a delivery and loading area should be controlled and restricted to identified and
authorised personnel only (i.e. verify the identity of the delivery man before granting access).
[Baseline]
7. All delivered materials should be inspected to ensure that there are no explosive, chemicals or
other hazardous materials. [Baseline]
8. Additional physical access controls such as 2FA (i.e. biometric authentication/smart card +
password) must be implemented for sensitive areas. [Advanced]
9. Interlocking doors system should be installed at the entrance of sensitive areas to provide extra
security. [Advanced]
10. If applicable, identification badges should be worn by all company staff within office premises.
The company logo or name should not be printed on the badges. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Termination of Physical Access Rights
Nội dung chính: [Termination of Physical Access Rights]
1. Termination of physical access rights must involve all physical access methods (i.e. swipe cards,
keys) being returned or disabled within the same business day. [Baseline]
2. Physical access rights must be terminated or adjusted whenever a staff leaves the company or
changes his/her job role. [Baseline]
3. Visitors must be asked to surrender their temporary permits before leaving the site. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Termination of Physical Access Rights

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Physical Security Controls
Nội dung chính: [Physical Security Controls]
1. All keys, smart cards, passwords, etc. for entry to restricted areas must be physically secured and
managed by authorised personnel such as the Premises Department. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Physical Security Controls

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 48
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 48]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 48

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. Lost keys, smart cards or leaked passwords etc. must be reported to authorised personnel
immediately. Where applicable, the lost smart cards or affected accounts must be disabled and
the affected password must be changed using the access control system. [Baseline]
3. Cages and racks/cabinets within data centres must be locked at all times, unless they are required
to be opened for maintenance. [Baseline]
4. Closed-circuit television that can record people entering and leaving sensitive areas and the
delivery and loading area should be implemented. The view of cameras should cover the whole
area. Recordings should be stored for at least three months for possible future playback. [Baseline]
5. Perimeter security system or alarms must be installed and tested at least yearly to cover the office
premises. Areas not in use must be locked at all times, regardless of how long the period might
be. [Baseline]
6. There should be no sign, or any sorts of indication located outside or inside the building suggesting
the location of sensitive information or company assets. [Baseline]
7. Photography, video or audio recording equipment should not be allowed in sensitive areas unless
authorised. [Baseline]
8. A minimum amount of paper and electronic media should be kept on the working desk. All
physical media with sensitive information should be locked in storage while not in use or after
office hours. [Advanced]
9. Point of Interaction (POI) devices that capture payment card data via direct physical interaction
with payment cards must be protected from tampering and unauthorized substitution, including
via the following controls: [PCI]
o Maintaining a list of POI devices.
o At least weekly inspecting POI devices to look for tampering or unauthorized substitution.
Alternatively, defining the frequency must be determined via a formal risk analysis.
o Training personnel to be aware of suspicious behaviour and to report tampering or
unauthorized substitution of devices.
10. Physical and/or logical controls are implemented to restrict use of publicly accessible network
jacks within the facility. [PCI] [Advanced]
11. Physical access to wireless access points, gateways, networking/communications hardware, and
telecommunication lines within the facility is restricted. [PCI] [Advanced]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security
Nội dung chính: [B2. Environmental Security]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [B2.1. Policy Objective]
Environmental security controls provide physical protection against natural disasters, malicious attack or
accidents.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [B2.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to the company’s staff, offices and facilities.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [B2.3. Definition]
Critical areas for the company’s business include places such as data centres, server rooms, mainframe
computer rooms, cardholder data environment or other areas housing IT facilities supporting critical
business activities.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [B2.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > B2. Environmental Security > B2.4. Policy Statements > General Management
Nội dung chính: [General Management]
1. A list of key personnel and emergency responders must be identified to facilitate that appropriate
actions will be taken in case of emergency. All staff should also be reminded of the key activities
and escape routes during the awareness training as referenced to L2. Security Awareness.
[Baseline]
2. All emergency exits must be clearly marked. [Baseline]
3. The company’s environmental security controls must be tested and subjected to audits performed
by independent auditors at least yearly. [Baseline]
4. The access control to the environmental monitoring control room must be referenced to B1.
Physical Security. [Baseline]
5. Critical IT facilities should not be placed near external windows. [Baseline]
6. Eating, drinking and smoking should not be allowed in critical areas. [Baseline]
7. All IT equipment should be maintained in accordance with the supplier’s recommended service
intervals and specifications to make sure that the equipment can conform to the manufacturer’s
specifications. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
8. Maintenance and repair services must be performed by authorised and qualified personnel only.
[Baseline]
9. Records should be kept for all suspected or actual faults and their respective preventive and
corrective maintenance. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Power Supply and Electrical Requirement
Nội dung chính: [Power Supply and Electrical Requirement]
1. Redundant power supply from different electrical substations should be deployed for data centres.
[Baseline]
2. Redundant power cables to data centres that are physically separated should be deployed to
ensure that there is continuous power delivery in the event one of the cables is cut or otherwise
damaged. [Baseline]
3. Uninterruptible power supply (UPS) should be deployed to provide emergency power in case of
power outage, in particular for critical areas. [Baseline]
4. UPS should be placed in separate areas from the other IT facilities in data centres. [Baseline]
5. UPS should be tested at least quarterly. [Baseline]
6. UPS utilisation should be monitored. The system should be able to trigger alerts in case of
abnormal fluctuations. [Baseline]
7. Emergency lighting (backup lighting) for data centres should be installed. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Power Supply and Electrical Requirement

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Cabling Security
Nội dung chính: [Cabling Security]
1. Power cables should be segregated from communications cables to prevent interference.
[Baseline]
2. Power and telecommunications lines into the company’s information processing facilities should
be underground or subjected to adequate alternative protection (i.e. shielded and concealed) to
prevent tempering. [Baseline]
3. Cable patching records should be properly documented. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Cabling Security

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Temperature and Humidity Control
Nội dung chính: [Temperature and Humidity Control]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Temperature and Humidity Control

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 52
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 52]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 52

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
1. Automatic temperature and humidity controls must be deployed within the facility where the
information system resides at to detect fluctuations that are potentially harmful to the electronic
devices. [Baseline]
2. If any fluctuations or anomalies are detected, the staff on site should closely monitor the situation
and decide if it is necessary to call for external support to fix the issues. Incident response
procedures listed inside the I1. Incident Response should be followed. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Fire Control
Nội dung chính: [Fire Control]
1. Fire detection system must be deployed in all areas. Fire suppression system should also be
installed to minimize the damage resulting from the fire. [Baseline]
2. Fire suppression system that can minimize the damage on electronics or other valuable assets
such as fire protection fluid or FM200 should be used instead of water sprinklers. [Baseline]
3. Regular fire drills must be carried out at least annually for the company’s staff to practice the
routines to be followed in case of a fire. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Fire Control

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Flood Control
Nội dung chính: [Flood Control]
1. All equipment should be located away from overhead water pipes, air conditioning ducts,
washrooms and pantry areas etc. to prevent potential water damage. [Baseline]
2. Water leakage detection system must be deployed in critical areas to detect water leakage.
[Baseline]
3. Master water shutoff valves should be installed and activated in case water leakage or flooding
has been detected to minimize the damage resulting from the water. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Flood Control

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Lightning Protection
Nội dung chính: [Lightning Protection]
1. Surge protector should be fitted to incoming power and communication lines. [Baseline]
2. Lightning rod should be installed to protect the building housing IT facilities from lightning strikes.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Lightning Protection

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Static Discharge Protection
Nội dung chính: [Static Discharge Protection]
1. Anti-static flooring should be implemented to protect the IT facilities inside data centres. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Static Discharge Protection

computers. [Baseline]

Nội dung chính: [C1.1. Policy Objective]
To ensure that all organizational assets are identified, documented and maintained to minimize the risks

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Ownership of Asset
Nội dung chính: [Ownership of Asset]
1. Asset ownership must be assigned when assets are created or acquired. [Baseline]
2. Asset Owners should define and document the classification of their assets (i.e. non-critical,
critical and highly critical) in the asset inventory according to the information that the asset would
store and process, along with the importance of the asset to the organization’s business
operations. The assets should be classified based on their criticality to the business: [Baseline]

to the C2. Asset Disposal before disposal when the assets have reached their end-of-life. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Acceptable Use of Asset
Nội dung chính: [Acceptable Use of Asset]
1. Users must adhere to the requirements stipulated in the Acceptable Use Policy. [Baseline]
2. All usage of non-licensed, shareware, freeware and pirated software should be reported to
relevant Asset Owner, Chief Information Officer and Risk Manager of the company such that
appropriate actions can be taken to prevent future infringements. [Baseline]
3. Normal users should be forbidden from installing applications on their company device. Only the
company’s IT Officers holding the local administrator account should be able to perform such
actions. For the management of administrative rights, please refer to the D1. Logical Access
Management. [Baseline]
4. Users should be forbidden from copying and transferring company owned assets. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Acceptable Use of Asset

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Return of Asset
Nội dung chính: [Return of Asset]
1. Users must return all organizational assets in their possession upon termination of their
employment, contract or agreement. [Baseline]
2. Before the company return any 3rd party assets to the service provider, the Asset Owners must
make sure that all company information stored inside the 3rd party assets are removed. [Baseline]
3. During the notice period of termination, the organization should ensure that unauthorized
copying of the organization’s data by terminated employees and contractors is prohibited.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Return of Asset

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > End-of-Life (EOL) / End-of-Support (EOS)
Nội dung chính: [End-of-Life (EOL) / End-of-Support (EOS)]
1. A review of the hardware and software technologies in use, including firmware, should be
performed at least once every 12 months to ensure that these remain secured and supported by
the vendor. This can be performed manually or by utilizing an asset management tool if available.
Reviews include, but are not limited to, reviews of licensing requirements and technologies that
are no longer supported by the vendor and/or no longer meet the security needs of the
organization. [Baseline][PCI]
2. IT asset should be replaced or upgraded to a supported version before reaching EOL/EOS.
[Baseline]
3. The use of EOL/EOS technology must be approved and should be regularly assessed for any
security risks (e.g., at least once a year). IT asset using EOL/EOS technology should be isolated in
a separate network segment. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > End-of-Life (EOL) / End-of-Support (EOS)

Nội dung chính: [C2. Asset Disposal]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [C2.1. Policy Objective]
Storage medium (i.e. electronic storage media and physical documents) containing sensitive information
should be securely destroyed, deleted or overwritten using techniques to prevent information leakage.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [C2.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of the company’s IT hardware and software and all of the company’s
information asset, including data in physical and electronic formats. This applies to assets stored in cloud.
Disposal/deletion of assets on public cloud should follow the F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [C2.3. Definition]
Assets that need to be securely disposed include storage media that stores data used for business
operations, including IT storage media such as hard drives, USBs, etc. and physical storage media such as
paper.
Cryptographic shredding is the practice of rendering sensitive data unreadable by deliberately by
overwriting or deleting encryption keys used to secure that data.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [C2.4. Policy Statements]
1. Disposal of devices and systems must be tracked in an asset inventory as per the C1. Asset
Management. [Baseline]
2. Where a disposal may subsequently be redeployed, all information relating to the previous
owner/use must be removed and recorded in the asset inventory. [Baseline]
3. For each disposal, IT Team should record the equipment (e.g., fill Asset Disposal Form) and it
should be approved by IT Operation Manager and update Finance and Administration department
(if needed) and audit trail for asset disposal should be maintained. [Baseline]
4. Removal of company information must be in line with local regulatory or legislative requirements.
[Baseline]
5. Where a third party is engaged for asset disposal, they must provide IT Asset Disposal Certificates
on a timely basis. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 60
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 60]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C2. Asset Disposal > C2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 60

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
6. Systems or devices that need not be retained must be disposed of in accordance with its
classification and associated disposal guidelines. [Baseline]
7. Prior to any equipment being disposed of, all hard drives must be removed from equipment
before disposal. Access to network devices, such as firewall and switch, should be removed and
network devices should be reset to factory default to erase all the configuration before disposal.
Equipment must be either physically destroyed or wiped to remove any confidential information
and licensed software. [Baseline]
8. Equipment and hard disk should be kept in protected environment before disposal. [Baseline]
9. Cryptographic shredding should be applied if the degauss and physical destruction is not available
(e.g., wiping SSD, cloud storage). This method should only be used if the encryption key is
managed by the company following K1. Cryptography and Technical Annex – Cryptography. It
should be noted that encryption strength can become weaker over time when computers get
faster or flaws are found, which may pose a risk to data disposed with cryptographic shredding.
[Baseline]
10. For disposal of enterprise grade storage device like SAN devices, the SAN device should be zeroed,
dissembled and the drives should be shredded. [Baseline]
11. Company information on paper must be securely destroyed when no longer required. Cross-
shredders should be used to shred company information and other sensitive information. These
shredders should be provided at convenient locations in offices, especially near printers and
photocopiers. [Baseline]
12. White boards should be cleaned at the end of meetings. Flipcharts or unwanted printouts of white
boards should be securely destroyed. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [C3.1. Policy Objective]
Removable and portable storage media should be managed to reduce the risk of information disclosure
and malware infection.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [C3.2. Scope]
This applies to all portable/removable storage devices, including USB flash drives, USB hard drives, storage
in mobile phones, backup tapes, and other devices that incidentally contain storage, like digital cameras.

Nội dung chính: [C3.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements > Use of removable storage device
Nội dung chính: [Use of removable storage device]
1. If the information to be taken out of the office is classified as “confidential” or “highly
confidential”, all removable storage device plugged in to workstations or laptops should be
encrypted with the support of volume base or content encryptions (e.g. Bitlocker will encrypt the
USB volume, SEE will encrypt the content). [Baseline]
2. C5. Information Security Handling should be followed when using removable storage device to
store company information. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements > Use of removable storage device

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements > Securing company workstations
Nội dung chính: [Securing company workstations]
1. Mobile devices from unknown source or origin should not be used unless it has been checked and
cleaned for computer virus and malicious code. [Baseline]
2. All sensitive information should be removed from the removable storage immediately after use.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements > Securing company workstations

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements > Securing important data on hosts
Nội dung chính: [Securing important data on hosts]
1. Company-authorized USBs should be disposed of in accordance with the C2. Asset Disposal.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements > Securing important data on hosts

Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 62]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C3. Removable Storage Media > C3.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 62

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. Changes in USB-related configuration on hosts should be logged and monitored in accordance
with A2. System Hardening. [Baseline]
3. When there is a business need for removable devices to be used to carry company information of
any data sensitivity classification, away from the office, this should be approved by a line
management, and the device should be supplied by the company with encryption enforced to
protect the information. [Baseline]
4. IT Team may consider disabling the USB port on hosts, where appropriate, and only enable the
USB port per business need with written approval. [Advanced]
5. Only company-authorized USBs should be used to copy/transfer data from hosts. [Advanced]
6. Company-authorized USBs should be recorded in accordance with the C1. Asset Management.
[Advanced]
7. Transfer of data from hosts to removable storage devices should be monitored. [Advanced]
8. DRM/DLP solutions should be in place to apply protection and encryptions on file level [Advanced]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C4. Information Security Classification > C4.1. Policy Objectives
Nội dung chính: [C4.1. Policy Objectives]
The information security classification guideline is integral to the foundation of an effective information
security programme. Without this classification system, it is impossible to know how to protect
company assets.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C4. Information Security Classification > C4.1. Policy Objectives

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C4. Information Security Classification > C4.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [C4.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all company’s information asset, including data in physical and electronic
formats.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C4. Information Security Classification > C4.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C4. Information Security Classification > C4.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [C4.3. Definition]
Sensitive data includes personal data and company information that could negatively impact the
company if leaked.
Personal data include personally identifiable information of customers, current and former employees,
contractors, and candidates for recruitment.
Sensitive personal data consists of racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical
beliefs, or trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data, data concerning health or data
concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation.

content:

Nội dung chính: [Classification Aggregation]
1. When information of more than one classification level is combined, the highest classification should
apply to the combined information. [Baseline]
2. The classification of an application, database, or server is equivalent to the highest classification of
any company information processed by the application or stored in the database or on the server.
[Baseline]

content:

Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
1. The classification level should be re-evaluated under new legal or regulatory requirements and
changed if necessary. The Information Owners should approve all changes to the classification level.
[Baseline]
2. All classified information should be labelled appropriately, whether the information is in electronic or
physical format. [Baseline]
3. Data inventories should be maintained and reviewed regularly to identify and record the classified
information maintained by the Information Owners. [Baseline]
4. DRM of File labelling software should be used to perform auto-labelling on digital documents
[Advanced]
5. Data inventories / records of processing activities should at least document the following information

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: [PDPO] [GDPR] [LGPD]:
Nội dung chính: [[PDPO] [GDPR] [LGPD]:]
• Information Owner / Department
• Data classification
• Category of record (e.g. personnel records, membership records, etc.)
• Items of personal data contained in the record
• Means of collection of the data
• Purpose of collection and use of data
• Retention period of the data
• Location for data storage
• Disclosure of data to any third parties including data processors and the names and relevant
details of third parties
• Possible location of transfer
• Purpose of disclosing the data and whether the disclosure complies with the regulatory
requirements
• Date of return or destruction by the data processor (if applicable)
• Security measures adopted
6. Data Protection Officer should be responsible for the review of personal data inventory and data
protection measures to ensure compliance with applicable data protection laws and regulations.
[GDPR] [LGPD] [PIPL] [PDP Law]
7. Risk assessments on handling activities of important data, determined as such by the corresponding
region and regulatory department, should be carried out periodically, e.g. annually, and the risk
assessment reports should be sent to the relevant regulatory departments. Risk assessment reports
should include the types and amounts of important data being handled; the circumstances of the data
handling activities; the data risks faced, methods for addressing them, and so forth. [DSL]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.1. Policy Objectives
Nội dung chính: [C6.1. Policy Objectives]
The intellectual property protection guideline provides information on the how to define intellectual
properties, and relevant security controls that the company should adopt to ensure the proper protection
of such properties and the use of proprietary software products.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.1. Policy Objectives

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [C6.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all company’s intellectual properties, information assets and staff.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [C6.3. Definition]
Intellectual property (IP) refers to creations of the mind, such as inventions, literary and artistic works,
designs, symbols, names, and images used in commerce. For example, industrial property includes patents
for inventions, industrial designs, trademarks and geographical indications, whereas copyright and related
rights cover literary, artistic and scientific works, including performances and broadcasts. IP rights are also
at the foundation of the software industry in which there are essentially 4 types of IP rights relevant to
software: patents, copyrights, trade secrets, and trademarks. Patents, copyrights and trade secrets can
be used to protect the technology itself, whilst trademarks do not necessarily protect technology, but the
names or symbols used to distinguish a product in the marketplace.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [C6.4. Policy Statements]
1. Proper access controls as referred to D1. Logical Access Management must be adopted by the
company to protect all intellectual properties. [Baseline]
2. Intellectual properties must be classified and handled in ways as referred to in C4. Information
Security Classification and C5. Information Security Handling. [Baseline]
3. All company software must be acquired only through official and reputable sources to ensure that
copyrights are not violated. [Baseline]
4. Appropriate asset registers must be maintained as referred to in C1. Asset Management and all
assets with requirements to protect IP rights must be identified. [Baseline]
5. Proof and evidence of ownership of licenses, master disks, manuals etc. must be maintained.
[Baseline]
6. The IT Team should keep track of the number of users for relevant licensed products such as
software tools or applications to ensure that the maximum number of users permitted within the
license is not exceeded. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C6. Intellectual Property Protection > C6.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [D1.1. Policy Objective]
Appropriate access control prevents unauthorized access and modifications of the company’s systems
and services.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [D1.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all system or application-level access. Physical, wireless network, remote
access and password related requirements should be referenced to the B1. Physical Security, H2. Wireless
Security, D3. Remote Access & Client VPN and D2. Password Security.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [D1.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements > General Access Management
Nội dung chính: [General Access Management]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements > General Access Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements > Access Provisioning
Nội dung chính: [Access Provisioning]
1. All user access must be granted based on the principle of least privilege, meaning that only the
access rights which are essential to the user’s intended roles and responsibilities should be
granted. [Baseline]
2. All accounts must have unique user lDs and passwords, for which the users themselves are
accountable for protecting their user credentials. [Baseline]
3. The end date for temporary access rights should be defined during the account creation process.
[Baseline]
4. In case a shared account must be used amongst various users, the account password must be
changed within every 30 to 60 days, or when one of the users leaves or changes to another job
role. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements > Access Provisioning

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 76
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 76]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D1. Logical Access Management > D1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 76

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
5. All user accounts and access rights should only be granted after obtaining written approval from
the Department Head. [Baseline]
6. Distribution of user credential to relevant staff for initial login upon account creation must be
performed via a secured channel. [Baseline]
7. Enforce user to change password upon successful login using default or initially created password.
[Baseline]
8. Approval records for access right changes must be maintained. The approval records should
include the following information: [Baseline]
o The request (application / modification / deletion);
o Relevant system / application;
o Name and position of the requester;
o Date of submitting the request;
o Name, position and signature of the approver;
o Date of granting the approval;
o Name of the IT Officer who is responsible for the technical procedures;
o Completion date
9. Access to non-user accounts must be requested, reviewed and approved by the company’s IT
Team. All access must be logged. [Baseline]
10. On any publicly accessible login portals to both internal and Internet-facing systems, users should
be presented with a notification such as login prompt stating that only authorised personnel can
log in or attempt to log in and all unauthorised access will be prosecuted under any applicable
laws. [Advanced]
11. The user is required to re-authenticate to re-activate the terminal or session after 15 minutes idle
time. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Removal or Adjustment of Access Rights
Nội dung chính: [Removal or Adjustment of Access Rights]
1. Access rights for users who have changed roles or left the company must be disabled or adjusted
within one business day. For users that have left the company, the user account must be removed
within one business month, or on a later date if there is a business requirement to defer the
deletion to a future, pre-defined date. [Baseline]
2. Temporary access rights granted to temporary staff, external parties or vendors must be set
expired date and disabled upon termination of their contract or agreement. Reactivation of the
account or extension beyond the originally advised end-date must be approved by authorised
personnel such as Department Head. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Removal or Adjustment of Access Rights

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Review of Access Rights
Nội dung chính: [Review of Access Rights]
1. The account lists maintained by the company for auditing purpose must include all account types,
including both user and non-user accounts. [Baseline]
2. Department Heads must review the user access rights at least annually to ensure that: [Baseline]
o only active users have access to the company’s systems and information;
o access rights are commensurate with the users’ duties;
o privilege creep gradual accumulation of access rights beyond what an individual needs to
do his or her job is not happened.
3. Auditing on the access rights and usage of non-user accounts must be performed by the
company’s IT Team at least annually. [Baseline]
4. User account should be disabled immediately if it has been idle for 90 days, or if it is known that
the account will not be required for a period of time (e.g. the account’s user is taking long-term
leave or has been seconded elsewhere). [Baseline]
5. All user accounts and related access privileges, including third-party/vendor accounts should be
reviewed at least once every six months. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Review of Access Rights

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Privileged Access Management
Nội dung chính: [Privileged Access Management]
1. Privileged access rights should be assigned to a user ID different from the general user accounts.
[Baseline]
2. Local administrative rights should not be assigned to end users. [Baseline]
3. Privileged user accounts should not be used to perform regular business or personal activities.
[Baseline]
4. Administrator account should not be shared amongst various users. If a shared administrator
account must be used, the password must be changed every time after each access. [Baseline]
5. All activities performed via the privileged user account must be logged and a sample set of
activities reviewed at least on a quarterly basis. [Baseline]
6. All privileged access must be requested and approved on a need-to-use basis. [Baseline]
7. For a more secured management over privileged access, a Password Management System and/or
a Privileged Access Management tool should be used to secure and protect all privileged account
passwords and log all access requests. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Privileged Access Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 78
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 78]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
8. If accounts used by systems or applications can be used for interactive login, they should be
secured as follows: [Baseline][PCI]
• Interactive use should be disabled except when required in exceptional circumstances.
• Use of interactive login should include a defined timeframe.
• The business justification must be documented.
• Explicit approval from management should be obtained.
• Authorization of user identity must be performed before granting access.
• Transactions should be attributable to an individual user.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Usage of Privileged Utility Program
Nội dung chính: [Usage of Privileged Utility Program]
1. Usage of privileged utility program must be restricted to a minimum number of users from the
company’s IT Team. All usage must be properly requested, reviewed and approved by the IT
Manager. [Baseline]
2. The IT Manager must be notified ahead of all ad-hoc or emergency usage of privileged utility
program. A formal request and approval procedure along with the reason for the emergency
usage should be documented afterwards. [Baseline]
3. All usage must be logged and reviewed at least yearly. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D2. Password Security > D2.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [D2.1. Policy Objective]
It is required to establish a standard to securely maintain and manage passwords, e.g. creation of strong
passwords, the protection of those passwords, and the frequency of change.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D2. Password Security > D2.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [D2.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D2. Password Security > D2.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D2. Password Security > D2.4. Policy Statements > Password Usage Requirement
Nội dung chính: [Password Usage Requirement]
1. A password policy must exist which governs all operating system user and administrative accounts.
Its contents must include a minimum length, maximum lifetime, complexity requirements and
history setting for passwords. The following password policy requirements are mandatory
minimums: [Baseline]
o Password minimum length requirement:
o A minimum password length for general users of 8 characters [Baseline]. In a card
processing environment the minimum password length shall be 12 characters, or 8
characters if the system does not support 12 characters [PCI] ; Alternative controls to
meeting this requirement include but are not limited to:
o Implementing Passwordless Authentication
o Passwords should be screened against list of compromised passwords
o a minimum password length of 16 characters for privileged users;
o a minimum password length of 25 characters for services account for which
passwords are not changed every 90 days (e.g. SPN Services Account);
o a maximum lifespan of 90 days for any password (Except services account);
o enforced complexity requiring the use of upper-case letters (A-Z), lower case letters (a-z)
numeric characters (0-9) and special characters;
o a password history that prevents re-use of the user’s last twelve passwords;
o locking the account out after no more than five failed login attempts;
o a lock-out duration of at least 30 minutes, or until an administrator intervenes; and
o passwords must be salt and hashed before storage to ensure they cannot be recovered
using decryption tools.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D2. Password Security > D2.4. Policy Statements > Password Usage Requirement

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D2. Password Security > D2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 81
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 81]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D2. Password Security > D2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 81

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. If the password lifespan requirement cannot be applied to certain machine accounts, such as for
service accounts, an alternative password policy must at least include: [Baseline]
o The use of long passphrases (at least 25 characters) to eliminate the risk of brute force
attacks;
o Screening of all passwords against lists of known commonly used or compromised
passwords to prevent use of known weak, bad or compromised passwords;
o Locking the account out after no more than five failed login attempts; and
o Change passwords at least every 12 months.
3. The initial password assigned to the new user must be unique and must not be the same for every
new account; nor must it be based on a predictable pattern. [Baseline]
4. User accounts that have system-level privileges granted through group memberships or programs
such as sudo must have a unique password from all other accounts held by that user to access
system-level privileges. [Baseline]
5. All System-Level Passwords must be documented and secured in the password management
database with access controls implemented in accordance with D1. Logical Access Management.
[Baseline]
6. Individual passwords must not be communicated to others (including over the phone), shared,
written down in an insecure way. [Baseline]
7. Any password that has been/or is suspected of being exposed must be changed immediately.
[Baseline]
8. Any concern over suspected or actual malicious use of the access capability must be notified
immediately to the IT Helpdesk or Security Team. [Baseline]
9. Any default/initial password allocated to the individual's id (e.g. at initial set-up of the id, or when
the password is reset by the local Help Desk) must be changed immediately at the next logon. This
requirement is also applicable to default login credentials on third party systems. [Baseline]
10. Users should not display their password when they enter the password. [Baseline]
11. A self-service portal should be provided for users to reset their login credentials. [Advanced]
12. Passwords should be screened to filter out the known weak, or bad or compromised passwords
using cracking or protection tools such as Azure AD password protection. [Advanced]
13. Password-less authentication should be enabled to provide a more secured authentication.
[Advanced]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Password Protection
Nội dung chính: [Password Protection]
1. The effective management of the password is the responsibility of the individual user. [Baseline]
2. Password files must be stored separately from application system data. [Baseline]
3. Passwords should not be hard coded in scripts, configuration/property files, or custom source
code. [PCI] [Advanced]
4. All passwords are to be treated as highly confidential and must be protected (e.g. salted and
hashed) against reverse engineering attacks. [Baseline]
5. Users should acknowledge receipt of secret authentication information. [Baseline]
6. Users must not transmit passwords in clear text via a network. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Password Protection

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Multi-factor Authentication
Nội dung chính: [Multi-factor Authentication]
1. Authenticating to cloud-based services or for remote access to corporate systems such as VPN or
webmail must use multi-factor authentication in addition to passwords to ensure strong
authentication in accordance with J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode.
[Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN
Nội dung chính: [D3. Remote Access & Client VPN]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [D3.1. Policy Objective]
It is required to establish a standard to minimize the potential exposure from unauthorized use of
corporate resources when connecting to the corporate network from outside the workplace.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [D3.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all company networks.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [D3.3. Definition]
A Virtual Private Network allows user to create a secure connection to another network over the Internet
and minimize the potential exposure from damages which may result from unauthorized use of corporate
resources. Damages include the loss of sensitive or company confidential data, intellectual property,
damage to public image, damage to critical internal systems, etc.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [D3.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.4. Policy Statements > Remote Connecting to Company Network
Nội dung chính: [Remote Connecting to Company Network]
1. ln any situation where a company network must be accessed from a remote location (either by a
member of staff who is off-site, or by another network), a virtual private network (VPN) must be
implemented. [Baseline]
2. VPN access must be granted on a need-to-use basis. [Baseline]
3. After 30 minutes of inactivity, VPN users must be automatically disconnected from the network.
The user must log in again to re-establish access to the VPN. Artificial methods of keeping the
connection open (e.g. pings) must not be used. [Baseline]
4. VPN users, and staff who are responsible for granting VPN user accounts, must meet the
requirements in the D2. Password Security and the D1. Logical Access Management. [Baseline]
5. All authentication attempts must be logged. VPN logs should be reviewed weekly to detect
abnormal login activities. [Baseline]
6. VPN authentication must be multi-factor (and if the VPN provides access to a cardholder data
environment, must be multi-factor). This could involve tokens (hardware or software, e.g. Google

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.4. Policy Statements > Remote Connecting to Company Network

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 84
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 84]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > D3. Remote Access & Client VPN > D3.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 84

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
Authenticator), biometrics, or simply a client-side certificate file that identifies the host being
used. [Baseline]
7. VPN clients must ensure that the computer on which they are running cannot be used as a bridge
to the company’s network. [Baseline]
8. VPN Split tunnelling is not permitted. [Baseline]
9. Users should avoid using untrusted network for VPN connection. [Baseline]
10. Host compliance check should be performed to ensure the security of the VPN connection.
[Advanced]
11. Administrative services (e.g. RDP, SSH) should not be directly exposed to the Internet, if remote
access is required, tools that use "mediation servers" can be used. Examples include LogMeln and
Any Desk. The use of TeamViewer should be avoided. [Advanced]
12. Log analysis tools can be used to monitor and scan VPN logs automatically. [Advanced]
13. Suitable minimum levels of technology and encryption must be used when implementing VPNs.
For client-to-network VPNs, L2TP should be used in preference to PPTP (although the latter is
permissible). Permitted secure protocols include IPSEC, SSL/TLS and OpenVPN. Other proprietary
protocols should be avoided unless a risk assessment has been carried out. [PCI]
14. Technical controls should be in place when using remote-access technologies to prevent copy
and/or relocation of PAN for all personnel, except for those with documented, explicit
authorization. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Use of Remote-control Facility
Nội dung chính: [Use of Remote-control Facility]
1. All hosts making use of VPNs, company’s devices or personal, must be up to date with patches
and anti-malware tools, and should be running a personal firewall. [Baseline]
2. VPN quarantine must be used to prevent computers that do not meet this requirement from
having full access to the company's network. [Baseline]
3. By using VPN technology with personal equipment, users must agree and understand that their
equipment is a de facto extension of company’s network, and such are subjected to the same
rules and regulations that apply to company owned equipment. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Use of Remote-control Facility
