content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery
Nội dung chính: [E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery > E1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [E1.1. Policy Objective]
Companies should have workable business continuity plans in place to protect all the critical areas of their
business and to cope with prolonged disruptions. Companies should also have workable IT disaster
recovery plan and procedures to ensure that services can be resumed within a short period of time and
in accordance with the business recovery requirements.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery > E1.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery > E1.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [E1.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all business operations of the company that is supported by IT systems.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery > E1.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery > E1.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [E1.3. Definition]
• Business continuity planning refers to the advance planning and preparations which are necessary
to identify the impact of potential losses arising from an emergency or a disaster; to formulate
and implement viable recovery strategies; to develop recovery plans which ensure continuity of
a company’s business operations in that relation; and to administer a comprehensive testing and
maintenance programme.
• Business continuity plan (BCP) refers to a collection of procedures and information which is
developed, compiled and maintained in readiness for use in the event of an emergency or disaster.
• Business impact analysis (BIA) refers to a management level analysis which identifies and assesses
the impact of losing the various functions and business operations within a company. The impact
analysis tries to measure the potential loss and escalating losses over time in order to provide
senior management with reliable data for the identification of critical services. Based on the
results of the analysis, the company should be able to identify the scope of the critical services to
be provided and the timeframe in which the services should be resumed.
• Recovery strategy refers to a strategy to resume the minimum set of critical services identified in
the business impact analysis.
• Disaster recovery plan (DRP) refers to a collection of procedures and information which is
developed, compiled and maintained in readiness to recover orderly and timely from an
interruption of data processing and/or network services.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery > E1.3. Definition

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E1. BCP and Disaster Recovery > E1.4. Policy Statements

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Board and senior management oversight
Nội dung chính: [Board and senior management oversight]
1. The Board of Directors and senior management of the company have the ultimate responsibility
for business continuity planning and the effectiveness of their BCP and ICT Continuity Plans.
Management approval should be obtained for both plans. The ICT Continuity Plan includes the
following: [Baseline]
• Performance and capacity specifications to meet the business continuity requirements
and objectives as specified in the BIA;
• Recovery time objective (RTO) of each prioritized ICT service and the procedures for
restoring those components
• Recovery point objective (RPO) of the prioritized ICT resources defined as information and
the procedures for restoring the information.
2. The senior management should establish policies, standards and processes for business continuity
planning, which should be endorsed by the Board. [Baseline]
3. The senior management should ensure that business continuity planning is taken seriously by all
levels of staff and that sufficient resources are devoted to implementing the plan. [Baseline]
4. The senior management should establish clearly which function in the institution has the
responsibility for managing the entire process of business continuity planning (the BCP function).
[Baseline]
5. The BCP function should submit regular reports to the Board and senior management on the
testing of its BCP. [Baseline]
6. Any major changes to the BCP should also be reported to the senior management. [Baseline]
7. The BCP should be reviewed annually by the company, with consideration of lesson learnt from
BCP drill and the changing risk profile of the company. The internal audit function of the company
or Group (if the company does not have an internal audit function) should conduct review of the
company’s BCP controls with defined audit cycle. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Board and senior management oversight

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Business impact analysis
Nội dung chính: [Business impact analysis]
1. BIA should at least include the following [Baseline]:
• Identify the different kinds of risks to business continuity and quantify the impact of
disruptions;
• Identify the critical services that must be maintained and continued in the event of a
disaster; and
• Determine how quickly the company needs to resume the critical functions or business
operations identified, and the corresponding RTOs and RPOs.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Business impact analysis

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 89
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 89]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 89

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. Based on the BIA results, the priority and required resources for the resumption of critical
functions should be determined. [Baseline]
3. The results of the BIA, especially the RPOs and RTOs, should be included in the BCPs. [Baseline]
4. BIA should be conducted at least annually by representatives of each business function of the
company and when there is any new business operation by individual business and support
functions, with the assistance of the BCP function. [Baseline]
5. Tabletop exercise should be conducted at least annually to enable key personnel with
emergency management roles and responsibilities to rehearse or discuss their duties in
simulated disaster scenarios. Scenarios should vary across test cycles. Examples of scenarios
include: [Baseline]
• Data loss or data breach, e.g. ransomware, deleted files, server/drive crash, stolen/lost
information asset
• Cyberattack, e.g. phishing, DDoS, malware, zero-day, compromised accounts or
machines
• Data centre outage
• Power outage
• Network outage
• Service disruption, e.g. natural disasters, fire, pandemic
• Breach notification from vendor
• Breach detected that requires vendor involvement for investigation and remediation

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Business continuity plan
Nội dung chính: [Business continuity plan]
1. A BCP should be developed with related teams/functions for all critical functions or business
operations identified during the BIA. [Baseline]
2. The BCP should be tested after any change to the business continuity process in the plan and
should be tested at least annually. Stakeholders should review the BCP results and see any area
need to improve. [Baseline]
3. BCP related document should be ready for staff when needed (e.g., available online and offline
in DR/alternative sites). [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Business continuity plan

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Disaster recovery plan
Nội dung chính: [Disaster recovery plan]
1. Disaster recovery plan (DR plan) should be developed for the disaster recovery of IT systems that
support the critical functions or business operations identified in the BIA. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Disaster recovery plan

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 90
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 90]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 90

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. DR procedure should be available for staff when needed (e.g. available online and offline in DR/
alternative site). [Baseline]
3. The DR drill should be conducted at least annually and whenever there is any change in production
network infrastructure to test the DR plan. [Baseline]
4. The DR drill result must meet the business continuity requirements determined from the BIA, i.e.
RTO(s). Results should be reviewed by stakeholders and see any improvement needed. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Alternate sites
Nội dung chính: [Alternate sites]
1. Alternate site should be readily available and working within the time requirement specified in
the BCPs. [Baseline]
2. Alternate site for technology recovery should be separate from the primary IT site. [Baseline]
3. Alternate site should have adequate physical measures in accordance with the B1. Physical
Security. This includes the use of public cloud services. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Alternate sites

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management
Nội dung chính: [F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [F1.1. Policy Objective]
The risks associated with third parties and vendors must be assessed and properly managed to ensure
that the company’s systems and information are appropriately accessed and protected.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [F1.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of the company’s external service providers and vendors.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.2. Scope
JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [F1.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.4. Policy Statements > Vendor Selection
Nội dung chính: [Vendor Selection]
1. Based on the outsourcing services required and confidentiality of the information involved, the
company should formulate and define the security requirements, as referenced to all relevant
Information Security Guidelines of the company, on the systems and information to be
outsourced or accessed by the vendors, including Information and Communication Technology
(ICT) providers. Such requirements must be clearly documented on the tender documents.
[Baseline]
2. ICT product suppliers should provide information describing the software components used in
products. In addition, ICT product suppliers should provide information describing the
implemented security functions of their product and the configuration required for its secure
operation. Sample documentation includes but are not limited to Software identification (SWID)
tags and System Documentation. This is an evolving area so additional guidance are expected to
be released for this requirement. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.4. Policy Statements > Vendor Selection

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 93
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 93]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management > F1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 93

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
3. Vendors should be required to submit their external audit reports such as ISO 27001 or SOC 2
report to the company such that their security controls can be assessed before vendor
engagement. [Baseline]
4. Background check and screening process on the external project team should be managed by the
project manager, as referenced to the L1. Personnel Security. [Baseline]
5. Due diligence should be performed before selecting a vendor. In assessing a vendor, apart from
the cost and quality of services, the company should evaluate the vendor’s current cybersecurity
posture and program for continuing improvements to assess its ability to meet the company’s
security and compliance needs over the duration of the business relationship. [Baseline]
6. The roles and responsibilities related to the use and management of third party services,
especially cloud, should be defined by the IT Manager/System Owner. There should be a clear
distinction which information security controls are managed by the service provider, and which
are managed by the organization as the service customer. The responsibility for controls must
be documented in a responsibility matrix which must be reviewed at least annually. [Baseline]
[PCI]
Enforcement of the Company’s IT Security Policy and Standards
1. Relevant parts of the company’s IT security policies, standards and procedures must be applied
to external parties with equal force and scope as to internal staff. All contracts between the
company and external service providers must contain appropriate clauses to protect the security
of the company's information and be subjected to legal review and sign off. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Contracting
Nội dung chính: [Contracting]
1. Service contracts between the company and the external party must be in place to administer the
followings at a minimum: [Baseline]
o Security controls requirements
The contract must clearly state the systems and information to be accessed by the
external party. Proper access control, data handling and protection, physical security,
business continuity controls, risk-based monitoring for anomalous activities,
implementing malware monitoring and protection solutions, providing dedicated support
in the event of an information security incident, supporting the organization in gathering
digital evidence, taking into consideration laws and regulations for digital evidence across
different jurisdictions, etc., must be in place for the external party, taking relevant legal
and regulatory requirements into consideration;
o Roles and responsibilities
Roles and responsibilities for the external party regarding the protection of the company’s
systems and information must be in place. This should also include the escalation process

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Contracting

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 94
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 94]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 94

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
for problem resolution and incident response (the requirements should be referenced or
in line with the I1. Incident Response), and the liabilities in such information security
incidents. The external party should also be responsible for providing a list of their sub-
contractors, if any, and to ensure that relevant trainings, if needed, are provided to both
their internal staff and sub-contractors to ensure that they can meet the security controls
requirements listed by the company;
o Regular auditing
The company should hold the rights to audit the external party and assess and their
security controls used to protect the company's systems and information;
o Service Level Agreement
A set of service level agreements (SLAs) should be included to define the expected
performance for each required security control, describe measurable outcomes, and
identify remedies and response requirements for any identified instance of non-
compliance;
o Indemnity against Damage or Loss
Appropriate and effective indemnity clauses should be included for external parties to
protect the company from damage or loss resulting from disruption of services or
malpractice of contractors' staff.
o Terms on Contract Termination
Termination clauses should be included to outline the conditions and potential reasons
of terminating the contract, instructions for how and when to notify the other party
regarding the contract termination, and all required actions upon contract termination
such as returning or destroying all company data kept by the external party, returning all
company assets in their possession, the time frame for such actions etc.
2. The service contracts must be reviewed by business users, Cyber Security Team, legal counsel and
approved by the management from each respective team. [Baseline]
3. For services rendered by external parties which involves access to the company’s physical
environment, systems or networks containing or processing Confidential and Highly Confidential
data, vendors must sign a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) to protect the company’s information.
[Baseline]
4. Interconnection agreements for connections between the organization’s network and third
party’s network should be established. [Baseline]
5. Pre-contract physical site visits of high-risk vendors should be conducted by the company’s Cyber
Security Team or by a qualified third party. [Advanced]

6. A continuous process improvement programme for third party due diligence activities should be
established. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Monitoring and Review of Outsourcing Service
Nội dung chính: [Monitoring and Review of Outsourcing Service]
1. The following processes should be in place to manage the delivery of outsourcing services:
[Baseline]
o The users of the outsourced services should monitor all external parties and vendors’
operations performance and service delivery according to the SLAs on a continuous basis;
o A list of all third-party service providers (TPSPs) with which account data is shared or that
could affect the security of account data must be maintained, including a description for
each of the services provided.
o Results of the service delivery should be documented, and regular meetings held by
relevant parties (i.e. business users, IT Department) should be conducted at least
quarterly to discuss and review the results;
o Review security issues, operational problems, security audit reports and follow up on
issues identified;
o Information is maintained about which PCI DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP,
which are managed by the entity, and any that are shared between the TPSP and the
entity. [PCI]
2. External / internal audit reports, information security control documents and other relevant
supporting evidence from the external parties and vendors must be collected and reviewed by
the company at least yearly or before the renewal of service contract to assess whether their
security controls are in line the company’s standards, to quantify the risk associated with the
external parties and to ensure that all risk items are properly managed. [Baseline]
3. In case there is an SLA breach, the IT Department and the users should consult the company’s
legal counsel to determine whether penalties should be taken, the magnitude of such penalties.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Monitoring and Review of Outsourcing Service

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Contract Expiry or Termination
Nội dung chính: [Contract Expiry or Termination]
1. The company must ensure that all company data and information in external services or facilities
are cleared or destroyed upon the expiry or termination of the service. Such destruction process
must comply with the company’s security requirements in accordance with its data classification.
[Baseline]
2. External service providers must return all company assets in their possession upon termination of
their services to the company. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Contingency Measures
Nội dung chính: [Contingency Measures]
1. Resilience and if necessary, recovery and contingency measures should be defined and
implemented to ensure the availability of the vendor’s information and information processing
and hence the availability of the company’s information. [Baseline]
2. A process for identifying and documenting product or service components that are critical for
maintaining functionality should be in place. Results should be incorporated in the BIA
documentation. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [G1.1. Policy Objective]
To ensure that security is an integral part of the company’s information systems across the entire system
development life cycle.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [G1.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to the company’s system development process and all relevant staff. The
system development process covers both new systems and existing systems (i.e. applying new features
or components to existing systems).

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [G1.3. Definition]
System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) is a process for planning, designing, building, testing, deploying and
maintaining an information system.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [G1.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > G1. System Development Life Cycle > G1.4. Policy Statements > System Specification and Design Control
Nội dung chính: [System Specification and Design Control]
1. A threat model should be built together with the development of the functional requirements
specifications to ensure that threat mitigations are present in all design and functional
specifications. A minimal threat model can be built by analysing high-risk entry points and data in
the application. [Baseline]
2. The IT Development Team and Cyber Security Team must review the system specifications and
design to ensure that the system is equipped with application controls to enforce adequate
authentication, authorization, accountability, confidentiality, integrity and availability. [Baseline]
3. Representatives from the IT Development Team and Cyber Security Team should review the
functional requirements specifications with the users to confirm the requirements and check if
there are any loopholes in maintaining the integrity of information. The users should be
encouraged to suggest corrective measures on any deficiency detected. [Baseline]
4. Representatives from the IT Development Team and Cyber Security Team should evaluate with
the users regarding the sensitivity of their data. The following information should be discussed:
[Baseline]
o Level of security to be achieved;
o Origin of the data source;

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 100
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 100]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 100

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
o Control of access to the development environment;
o Monitoring of change to the program code and the development environment;
o Secure storage of backups at offsite locations;
o Trustworthiness and security of 3rd party software components used in development,
including open source software;
o Control for data moving in and out of the environment.
2. System development documentation, program source code and listing of applications must be
properly maintained and restricted for access on a need-to-know basis. [Baseline]
3. System development should be performed on a system development environment (SDE) that is
separated from the production and testing environment. [Baseline]
4. Application integrity must be maintained through version control mechanism and separation of
environments for development, system testing, acceptance testing and live operation. [Baseline]
5. Web sites or mobile apps that load and execute payment page scripts in the consumer's browser
must meet the following: [PCI]
• A method is implemented to confirm that each script is authorized.
• A method is implemented to assure the integrity of each script.
• An inventory of all scripts is maintained with written justification as to why each is
necessary.
This requirement applies to all scripts loaded from the entity’s environment and scripts loaded
from third and fourth parties.
6. Users with access to the development environment should not be granted access to the
production environment unless explicitly approved by the System Owner and all user activities
must be closely monitored and logged. [Baseline]
7. For highly sensitive systems, the programs dealing with sensitive information should be divided
into different units of modules and segments and assigned to several programmers. [Baseline]
8. Source code review must be performed throughout the system development, i.e. as soon as the
code have been created or after remediating security bugs etc. to minimize the change of having
faulty code or programming fraud. [Baseline]
9. Program source code can be stored centrally as program source libraries and the following items
should be in place to control access to the libraries: [Baseline]
a. Program source libraries should not be held in production system;
b. All accesses to program source libraries should be recorded in audit log and maintained;
c. Strict change control procedures as referenced to the A6. IT Change Control must be
followed when maintaining the program source libraries.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 101
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 101]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 101

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
10. If public source code repositories such as GitHub, GitLab, Git, Bitbucket, Jenkins etc. are used, the
source code must not be shared with the public community and proper access controls must be
put in place to restrict access to the development team only. [Baseline]
11. Code analysis, security code scan or other security testing within an integrated development
environment (IDE) should be leveraged during the system development process. [Advanced]
12. Software code executables and scripts should be digitally signed to confirm the software author
and guarantee that the code has not been altered or corrupted. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > System Testing
Nội dung chính: [System Testing]
1. All system testing should be performed on a testing environment to prevent any potential
disruption on the production environment. [Baseline]
2. The configurations of the testing environment should be as similar with the production
environment as possible such that the testing results can accurately reflect the outcomes as if the
product has been launched on the actual production environment. [Baseline]
3. Unit test and system integration test should be performed before the user acceptance test (UAT)
to ensure that both the internal operation of a program and the system as a whole perform
according to specification. [Baseline]
4. Stress test or load test should be performed before the UAT to determine the stability and
maximum capacity of the system, which is essential for the company’s business continuity
planning and disaster recovery planning. [Baseline]
5. Security testing (e.g., vulnerability scanning and penetration testing) should be performed based
on internationally recognised security best practices such as OWASP Top 10 to determine whether
the security features built within the system and/or API and can operate as intended. [Baseline]
6. User acceptance test should be performed to ensure that expected results can be produced by
the system and/or API. The business users are responsible in preparing the test data, test plan
and performing the functional testing, including but not limited to the followings: [Baseline]
o Valid and invalid combinations of data and cases;
o Data and cases that violate the editing and control rules;
o Cases for testing the rounding, truncation and overflow resulting from arithmetic
operations;
o Cases for testing unexpected input, i.e. overly long input, incorrect data type, unexpected
negative values or date range, unexpected characters such as those used by the
application for bounding character string input etc.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > System Testing

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 102
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 102]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 102

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
7. Regression test should be performed to rerun a portion of test scenarios or test plan to ensure
that the changes or corrections deployed have not introduced new errors to the system or API.
[Baseline]
8. Test records stating the content and purpose of the test must be documented and reviewed by
both business and IT representatives. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Protection of Test Data
Nội dung chính: [Protection of Test Data]
1. Test data must be carefully selected, protected and controlled commensurate with its
classification. [Baseline]
2. Production data must be sanitised if they are to be used for testing purposes. [Baseline]
3. Operational databases containing personal or classified information should not be used for testing
purposes. If this cannot be avoided, the process must be properly documented, and the request
must be reviewed and approved by the Information Owner. The following controls should be
applied: [Baseline]
o Isolate the testing environment with strict access control;
o Personal data should be de-personalised before use;
o Classified information should be removed or modified beyond recognition before use;
o All these data must be cleared immediately after testing.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Protection of Test Data

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > System Deployment
Nội dung chính: [System Deployment]
1. The System Owner and IT Development Team should evaluate with the users on how they will be
affected if there is a loss to the data processing capability. A contingency plan should be
formulated following the evaluation. [Baseline]
2. Approvals from the company’s management, System Owner, IT Department and business users
must be obtained prior to the official system deployment. [Baseline]
3. Change management procedures as referenced to the A6. IT Change Control must be followed.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > System Deployment

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > System Maintenance
Nội dung chính: [System Maintenance]
1. The performance and security risk regarding the system must be closely monitored by the System
Owner and the users. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > System Maintenance

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 103

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. Regular system maintenance and upgrade should be performed to maintain the functionality,
security and performance of the system. Relevant requirements listed inside the A3. Vulnerability
Management, A6. IT Change Control and A1. Patching must be followed. [Baseline]
3. An inventory of third party frameworks, libraries and other code used in applications should be
tracked for security patches and updated in a timely manner as per A3. Vulnerability Management.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Development Team
Nội dung chính: [Development Team]
1. Software development personnel working on bespoke and custom software must be trained at
least once every 12 months regarding the following: [Baseline]
• On software security relevant to their job function and development languages.
• Secure software design and secure coding techniques.
• If security testing tools are used, how to use the tools for detecting vulnerabilities in
software.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security
Nội dung chính: [H1. Network Security]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [H1.1. Policy Objective]
The perimeter security of the network must be adequately protected against all known cyberattacks.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [H1.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all company systems and network.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [H1.3. Definition]
Unified Threat Managers (UTMs) are firewall products that combine the functionality of anti-virus
scanners, spam detectors, intruder detection/prevention systems, and content management and control
into a single platform that also performs traditional packet-filtering.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [H1.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.4. Policy Statements > General
Nội dung chính: [General]
1. There must be a firewall installed at each Internet connection or Network Perimeter,
development and test networks, intranet connections between business units, and between any
DMZ and the internal network. [Baseline]
2. The "Principle of Least Privilege " must apply to all firewalls and routers with access-control lists:
That which is not expressly permitted is denied. [Baseline]
3. Firewall administration and maintenance must only be carried out by designated staff or
contractors, who have received adequate training in the firewall product(s). [Baseline]
4. Administrative access to the firewall must originate only from the internal network and must use
strongly encrypted protocols (e.g. SSH or HTTPS). Each administrator should have his/her own
named user account on the firewall so that configuration changes can be traced back to the
individual who made them. [Baseline]
5. All changes to firewall rules must be checked for correctness before and after they have been
applied by a different firewall administrator (or appropriate colleague in smaller operations) to
the one applying them. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.4. Policy Statements > General

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 106
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 106]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H1. Network Security > H1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 106

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
6. An accurate and up-to-date documentation of the network components must be kept., including
the components listed below. The documents should be reviewed and sign off by the IT Manager
every 6 months. [Baseline]
• Network diagram showing network components, segmentation and connection to other
networks
• Firewall configuration, including network connection diagrams
7. The firewall administrators must be kept informed about actual or proposed changes to the
network so that the impact on the firewall can be assessed. [Baseline]
8. Firewall logs must be kept showing, as a minimum, any failed administrative log-in attempts, and
any packets that were denied while attempting to traverse the firewall from the internal network
or DMZ to the Internet. These logs must be retained for a reasonable amount of time (e.g. 3
months) to allow incidents to be investigated. Firewall log review should be performed every 3
months to identify malicious activities. [Baseline]
9. Detailed procedures governing the use of the firewall, procedures for amending rules, actions to
be taken in the event of an attack against the firewall (to include both hacking and denial-of-
service attacks) and guidance on detecting and avoiding attacks, should be produced and
distributed to all staff responsible (even tangentially) for the firewall. [Baseline]
10. All relevant security patches must be applied to firewalls in a timely manner, subjected to the
usual constraints of change-control and testing. [Baseline]
11. Risk assessments should be conducted to determine whether the company’s systems and portals
can be accessed via the public internet based on their criticality and sensitivity. [Baseline]
12. Logs of the network security devices should be integrated with SIEM tools for monitoring of
security threats. [Advanced]
13. Administrative passwords for the network security devices must be managed by Privileged
Identity Management tools or enterprise authentication system such as RADIUS and TACACS+,
must have a password length of at least 12, and password expiry should be less than or equal to
90 days. [Advanced]
14. All changes to firewall rules should follow standard change-control procedures. At the very least
they must be fully documented and authorised by the IT manager with responsibility for the
firewall before they are applied. [PCI]
15. A "security table" (usually a spreadsheet) that shows the expected configuration of the firewall
should be maintained, securely stored, and updated every time the firewall's configuration is
changed. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 107
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 107]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 107

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
16. Periodic testing of the firewall(s) should be carried out using automated testing tools or
penetration testing services from a group approved vendor. Such tests should include, as a
minimum, port scanning of all public IP addresses protected by the firewall to check that no new
ports have been opened up accidentally. [PCI]
17. The disclosure of internal IP addresses and routing information is limited to only authorized
parties. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Configuration
Nội dung chính: [Configuration]
1. The only inbound protocols (i.e. those that originate on the Internet) that are routinely permitted
through the group’s firewalls are SMTP, POP3, IMAP, HTTP, HTTPS, SCP and FTP, and traffic is
permitted only to appropriate servers. The following exceptions to this rule apply: [Baseline]
o where a site-to-site VPN is established between offices that comply with the IT security
policy;
o where staff access the network via the Internet using an authorised VPN product and
strong (preferably two-factor) authentication;
o where a suitable business case exists, providing that a risk assessment has been carried
out and no alternative approach is available.
2. All publicly accessible destination servers must be located in a secure DMZ. If a VPN is terminated
by a host other than the firewall, that host must be located in a secure DMZ. [Baseline]
3. Firewall rules governing outbound traffic (i.e. that which originates on the internal network)
should be as specific as possible, limiting the traffic only to certain protocols. [Baseline]
4. Firewall rules must be created on a need-to-use basis. Obsolete rules must be removed from the
firewall as soon as they are no longer needed. [Baseline]
5. Test rules must not be left in the rule base even if they are disabled. They must be removed and
added again later if they are required. [Baseline]
6. Firewalls, and the hosts that they protect, must be configured to be as stealthy as possible.
Inbound ICMP traffic (at least NETMASK and TIMESTAMP) must be dropped; ICMP ECHO traffic
(“pings”) may be permitted for diagnostic and support purposes but must be dropped if not
required. The firewall’s externally visible host name should be as anonymous as possible so that
no information is available about the type of firewall in use or the organisation who owns it.
[Baseline]
7. Firewall rules review should be conducted annually [Baseline], or at least every 6 months for card
processing environments [PCI].

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Configuration

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 108
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 108]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 108

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
8. Web Application Firewall (WAF) should be enabled. [Baseline]
9. Network segmentation should be implemented to separate assets hosting sensitive workloads
(e.g. management interface to IT infrastructure, security services (SIEM, PAM, etc.), cloud
management plane, etc.) from other networks. [Baseline]
10. Network Access Control (NAC) should be enabled. At a minimum, MAC address filtering should be
adopted. [Advanced]
11. All firewall rules must be as specific as possible in the definition of source and destination IP
addresses and protocols to be used. None of these values should be “any” (i.e. access to all
protocols/addresses) without a legitimate business reason. If “any” is used for testing or
debugging purposes, it must be amended to a more suitable value at the earliest possible
opportunity. [Baseline]
12. Once changes on firewall rules have been implemented, an external network vulnerability scan
should be carried out to ensure that they have not opened up any expected routes into the
network. Anything that looks unusual should be investigated. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Unified Threat Managers (UTMs)
Nội dung chính: [Unified Threat Managers (UTMs)]
1. Companies should deploy UTM with following features to safeguard the network perimeter:
[Baseline]
o Anti-Virus
o Anti-spam
o VPN
o Application Filtering
o IPS
o Web filtering
o Network Behaviour Monitoring
o DNS proxy services
o Network Time Protocol functionality
2. UTM solutions which offer next-generation firewalls can be used to protect the network security.
[Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Unified Threat Managers (UTMs)

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Network Perimeter IDS/IPS
Nội dung chính: [Network Perimeter IDS/IPS]
1. Intrusion Detection System (IDS) / Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) can be implemented as part
of a UTM or as a standalone system. Some form of IDS/IPS must be in place on any network that
is connected to the Internet or the Network Perimeter. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Network Perimeter IDS/IPS

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 109
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 109]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 109

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. Network-based IDS/IPS probes must be in a location where any suspicious traffic requires further
attention, e.g. monitoring traffic between a DMZ and the internal network. [Baseline]
3. The response to the receipt of the alert must be compliant with the guidelines set out in the I1.
Incident Response. [Baseline]
4. When the IDS/IPS monitoring is outsourced, the following requirements must be met: [Baseline]
o the service providers must follow the D1. Logical Access Management;
o records of false positives alert generated by the service providers must be kept and
reviewed;
o any major events not reported by the service providers must be investigated.
5. IDS/IPS logs must be retained for at least three months. [Baseline]
6. Host-based IDS/IPS or endpoint detection and response solution should be implemented on highly
critical servers on the internal network, particularly domain controllers, e-mail servers and web
servers. [Advanced]
7. Events generated by IDS/IPS tools must be handled according to the perceived severity of the
alert. The following handling procedure should be considered: [Advanced]
o the most serious alerts that require 24/7 response will trigger a procedure (either
internally or through an outsourcer) that results in a designated responsible staff member
being contacted by telephone; a second member of staff must be assigned to stand-by in
case the primary responder cannot be contacted. Whether or not 24/7 response is
provided should be based on the results of a risk assessment;
o less severe alerts generate a text or pager message to the primary responder;
o moderate alerts generate an e-mail to a list of responders;
o low-criticality alerts cause log entries to be created for review at a later date.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security
Nội dung chính: [H2. Wireless Security]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [H2.1. Policy Objective]
To protect against network attacks targeting the company’s wireless local area networks (WLAN).

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [H2.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to the company’s WLAN.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [H2.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.4. Policy Statements > Wireless Network Deployment
Nội dung chính: [Wireless Network Deployment]
1. All wireless networks provided for any purpose must be registered and approved by the
company’s Network Team. The SSID information and locations of respective access points should
be properly documented. Such documentations should be reviewed at least yearly or after any
major updates. [Baseline]
2. Network diagrams documenting the deployment of wireless networks must be in place. The
Network Team should ensure that there is no excessive coverage by the wireless signal. Wireless
signal test should be performed at least half-yearly. [Baseline]
3. All wireless network products must be sourced from corporate-approved vendors. Default
wireless access point configurations such as password, encryption keys, SNMP community strings
etc. must be changed prior to the deployment. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.4. Policy Statements > Wireless Network Deployment

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 111
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 111]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H2. Wireless Security > H2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 111

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
4. The application of staff accounts for connecting to the staff wireless network should be
referenced to the D1. Logical Access Management. For guests connecting to the guest wireless
network, a notification page should be prompted displaying the company’s Web and Internet
Usage Acceptable Use Policy. Guests should be required to accept the AUP before they can
connect to the guest network. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Technical Controls
Nội dung chính: [Technical Controls]
1. Wireless networks must not be connected directly to the company’s trusted internal network.
Access controls such as firewalls must be installed to segregate any wireless network and the
company’s network to which it is connected. The firewalls should be configured to only permit
authorised traffic necessary for business purposes between the wireless environment and the
company’s network. [Baseline]
2. All wireless access points must have unique administrator passwords and changed every 90 days
with reference to the D2. Password Security. [Baseline]
3. The SSID must not reflect any identifying information such as the company’s name, system or
product name / model etc. [Baseline]
4. All insecure wireless security protocols must be disabled. Detailed controls regarding the security
protocols should be referred to the Technical Annexes – Cryptography. [Baseline]
5. All wireless networks must be encrypted as referenced to the Technical Annex – Cryptography.
[Baseline]
6. Network Detection and Response (NDR) solutions should be deployed to protect the company’s
wireless networks. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Technical Controls

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Regular Testing
Nội dung chính: [Regular Testing]
1. Penetration test on the company’s wireless network must be performed at least yearly. [Baseline]
2. Scanning for rogue access points, WLAN controllers or unauthorized wireless networks must be
performed at least yearly. Any rogue devices or wireless networks identified must be reported to
the Network Team for follow-up. [Baseline] Scanning must be done quarterly for cardholder data
environments. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Regular Testing

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Physical Security
Nội dung chính: [Physical Security]
1. Physical access to the wireless access points and WLAN controllers must be restricted as
referenced to the B1. Physical Security. [Baseline]

Nội dung chính: [H3.1. Policy Objective]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H3. Domain Name System (DNS) Security > H3.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [H3.2. Scope]
This document applies to the company’s staff, contractual/part-time staff, and vendors.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H3. Domain Name System (DNS) Security > H3.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H3. Domain Name System (DNS) Security > H3.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [H3.3. Definition]
“Responsible team” is the in-house staff responsible for applying and implementing controls of DNS
throughout the technical design, development, system revamp, system integration, and daily operation.
“Service providers” are the external parties, such as third parties, agencies, contractors, and IT suppliers
responsible for designing, developing, or supplying services on behalf of Jardine.
“User” typically points to all the staff working for Jardine.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H3. Domain Name System (DNS) Security > H3.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > H3. Domain Name System (DNS) Security > H3.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [H3.4. Policy Statements]
1. A responsible team should be established to manage DNS and communicate with the Service
providers. [Baseline]
2. The domain name management should be secured based on this security policy, specifically the
requirement based on multi-factor authentication (MFA), least privilege principle, and the
access right review guidelines with reference to D. Access Control. [Baseline]
3. The policies and procedures of the domain name management lifecycle (from registration to
decommission) should be communicated to the relevant company personnel and service
providers. [Baseline]
4. The following security controls should be applied on DNS: [Advanced]
1. Enabling Domain Name System Security Extensions (DNSSEC) to protect the user from
the threat of fake domain information and against cache poisoning.
2. Enabling Domain-Based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance
(DMARC), to reduce the risk and protect users from email spoofing and phishing.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > The registration of the domain name
Nội dung chính: [The registration of the domain name]
1. The domain name registration should be endorsed and approved by the company’s legal team.
[Baseline]
2. The Domain name should be registered under the corporate entity, not by individual(s).
[Baseline]
3. Domain registrar(s) with considerable market size and good track records should be selected to
ensure service availability. [Baseline]
4. The domain name should be “locked” through the domain name management system,
preventing the domain from being transferred to an unauthorized account. [Baseline]
5. The domain name should be registered for a duration in accordance with the business
requirement. Generally, based on the reason that most domain names are registered in relation
to the brand, the maximum registration period should be selected from brand / intelligent
property (IP) right protection perspective. [Baseline]
6. The backup payment details and contact information should be provided to the domain register
to prevent losing the domain due to payment failures. [Baseline]
7. Registering variations of the organization’s domain names should be considered to reduce the
risk of phishing or domain name typo squatting. The following items should be considered for
registration of the variations of the organization’s domains: [Advanced]
a. Legacy generic Top-level Domain extensions, such as .com, .net and.org
b. Country code Top Level Domain extensions like .HK, .UK, US etc.
c. Brand and product Domain name
d. Common misspellings of the domain, such as yahoo.com, yaboo.com and ychoo.com
e. Domain extensions by the industry, such as:
i. Business: .group, .coop, .holdings
ii. Hospitality Event: .catering, .club, .guide
iii. Marketing, News and Communications: agency, .marketing, .media, .press

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > The registration of the domain name

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Operations
Nội dung chính: [Operations]
1. The responsible team should manage the registration status of all domain names. The master
list of the domain name shall be documented, protected, and reviewed annually. [Baseline]
2. The registrar account should be created and managed with reference to D2 Password Security.
[Baseline]
3. Only the responsible team should be authorized to carry out the domain name registration. The
responsible team should register, manage, and monitor DNS status on behalf of the user or
other department. [Baseline]
4. The responsible team should use MFA (or other equivalent / more secured means of
authentication provided by service provider) when accessing the domain name management
platform provided by service provider. [Baseline]
5. The responsible team should report to the senior management and legal team if the
organizational domain is purchased or taken over by a 3rd party. The responsible team should
not contact the Domain buyer/owner without senior management approval. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > DNS Monitoring
Nội dung chính: [DNS Monitoring]
1. The responsible team should be responsible for safeguarding, detecting, reporting, and
identifying the priorities of the monitoring issues of the internal or external domain. [Baseline]
2. The responsible team should report in the event of the hijacked domain or any unauthorized
usage, in accordance with the requirement in I. Information Security Incident Management.
[Baseline]
3. The responsible team, third parties, or service provider should report domain variants and the
domains used within the organization but not owned by the organization. [Baseline]
4. The domain name expiration date should be monitored and reported by the responsible team to
ensure domain registrations are renewed on time or allowed to expire if they are to be
decommissioned. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > DNS Monitoring

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Decommissioning
Nội dung chính: [Decommissioning]
1. Assessment should be performed prior to decommissioning, specifically risks related to “Domain
squatting” or “Phishing” attack, trademark protection and value of the domain name. [Baseline]
2. Following approval by Legal and IT teams, the domain decommissioning should be performed by
the responsible team and provide the appropriate HTTP redirections to a suitable URL.
[Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > I1. Incident Response > I1.4. Policy Statements > Definition of Incident
Nội dung chính: [Definition of Incident]
1. Assessments for each information security event must be conducted to decide if the information
security events are to be classified as information security incidents. Results of the assessment
and decision should be recorded for future reference and verification. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > I1. Incident Response > I1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 118

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
1. Situations to be considered for information security event investigation and reporting include:
[Baseline]
o Ineffective security control;
o breach of information integrity, confidentiality or availability expectations;
o human errors;
o external attacks;
o non-compliance with company policies and guidelines;
o violations of access control in the D1. Logical Access Management;
o breach of physical security arrangement;
o unauthorized network access;
o software malfunctions, where the symptoms of the problem and any messages appearing on
the screen.
o alerts from security monitoring systems such as:
• Intrusion-detection and intrusion-prevention systems.
• Network security controls.
• Change-detection mechanisms for critical files.
• The change-and tamper-detection mechanism for payment pages.
• Detection of unauthorized wireless access points.
2. In order to ensure that appropriate rectification action be taken promptly to cope with the
incident, the severity level of the incident should be assessed based on the actual/anticipated
impacts of the incident. [Baseline]
3. Adequate logging of system and user activity must be put in place to ensure any unusual activity
is highlighted. The requirements for logging and monitoring unusual activities must follow A5.
System Logging and Monitoring. [Baseline]
4. End-users must be in no doubt as to how to report an incident security event. A channel (e.g. a
phone hotline) must be available at all times and should be widely advertised to end-users.
Incidents should never go unreported because a user did not know who to tell, how to report, or
thought the process was too much trouble to justify the report. [Baseline]
5. Any user who suspects infection by a virus must disconnect from all networks, contact appropriate
IT support staff, and make no attempt to eradicate the virus, unless they do so while in
communication with a system administrator. [Baseline]
6. The procedure for dealing with a reported incident should be aligned with the Group’s Incident
Response Plan template or industry standards such as ISO27001 or NIST, and must include:
[Baseline]
• Plans, processes and procedures to contain, eradicate and recover from cyber incidents.
• Procedures to ensure IT and/or security staff are capable and have the appropriate
training on detection and response technologies and processes. Training for incident

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 119
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 119]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 119

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
response personnel should be conducted at least annually and after updates to the
Incident response plan.
• Businesses should determine whether more frequent training is required to address high
staff turnover or other factors as part of targeted risk analysis exercises. [PCI][Advanced]
• The accurate and timely reporting of incidents to relevant supervisory authorities,
regulators and other stakeholders.
7. If an incident involves an outbreak of a virus (where outbreak means more than a single isolated
incident), details should be communicated as soon as possible throughout the business unit so
that others may take appropriate preventative actions locally as required. [Baseline]
8. Cybersecurity SIG Teams, which contains a message board, on the main page, for reporting
security incidents, should be used to share incidents with other business units in the Jardine Group.
For emergency security incidents, Microsoft Teams (e.g. Cybersecurity SIG Teams) and Emergency
Security Contacts group (e.g. in Telegram) should be used to share the incident. [Baseline]
9. The incident response procedure should be reviewed and tested annually. [Baseline]
10. Group Cybersecurity must be promptly notified for any high severity incident. [Baseline]
11. The following situations and incident handling methods should be defined in the incident
response plan. [Advanced]
o If a member of staff is implicated in the outbreak, then the staff member’s line manager and
appropriate senior management will be immediately informed.
o If a third party is implicated, then a Director level member of that organisation will be
officially informed.
o Revoke access to IT Systems for the implicated staff member or third-party organization whilst
the suspicions are corroborated.
12. Quantitative and qualitative metrics should be established for incident escalation. [Advanced]
13. Incident response plan for payment card system breach should include: [Baseline]
o roles, responsibilities, and communication strategies in the event of a compromise including
notification of the payment brands, at a minimum;
o specific incident response procedures;
o business recovery and continuity procedures;
o data backup processes;
o analysis of legal requirements for reporting compromises;
o coverage and responses for all critical system components;
o reference or inclusion of incident response procedures from the payment brands.

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Forensic Readiness
Nội dung chính: [Forensic Readiness]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Forensic Readiness

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 120
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 120]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 120

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
1. All forensic investigation must be approved and managed by relevant Department Head and the
company’s Legal Department. [Baseline]
2. Roles and responsibilities of all personnel involved in the forensic investigation must be properly
documented. [Baseline]
3. All relevant evidence must be preserved and handled in a manner which can prove that the
information has not been altered since its recording. The technical team responsible for the
extraction of evidence should determine the most suitable method for different scenarios (i.e.
live / dead forensic image acquisition). [Baseline]
4. Strict access control must be carried out and only a minimum number of personnel should have
access to the container storing the digital evidence. Hashing must also be performed to ensure
that any evidence tempering would be detected. [Baseline]
5. All requests for the collection or handover and return of digital evidence must be properly
documented, reviewed and approved by the Information Owner, System Owner and Legal
Department. Records must be in place to track the entire chain of custody. [Baseline]
6. In case external investigators are required to access the corporate network or obtain the
company’s data, processes and procedures listed inside the F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management
must be followed. [Baseline]
7. The lead time for retrieving relevant logs should be subjected to the formal request. [Baseline]
8. System logs used as forensic evidence must be retained until the investigation or legal
proceedings are closed. [Baseline]
9. The environment containing the digital forensic evidence must be secured from unauthorised
access as soon as possible after the incident has been discovered to ensure that the contents
remain unchanged. [Baseline]
10. After the digital evidence have been returned by the investigator, all evidence and information
saved within the storage media must be disposed of with reference to the guidelines listed under

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C5. Information Security Handling and C2. Asset Disposal. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C5. Information Security Handling and C2. Asset Disposal. [Baseline] > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 121
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 121]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C5. Information Security Handling and C2. Asset Disposal. [Baseline] > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 121

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
12. The following system logs (where possible) and other evidence, but not limited to, should be made
available as forensic evidence with reference to relevant guidelines such as A5. System Logging
and Monitoring and A8. Data Backup: [Advanced]
o CCTV logs;
o System audit logs - security alerts, access logs, service logs;
o data backup and backup logs;
o removable media and Asset Register;
o network security logs - firewall, Anti-virus etc.;
o mobile phone and desk phone logs.
13. A quick analysis regarding the log files can be performed to get a basic understanding on the
incident. [Advanced]
14. Temporary files should be captured since they might be deleted after system shutdown.
[Advanced]
15. Two full backups should be created with one sealed and hashed for evidence and the other one
for additional backup. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Learning from Information Security Incidents
Nội dung chính: [Learning from Information Security Incidents]
1. Disciplinary action for violation of company's security policies and procedures must be included
in HR Policy. [Baseline]
2. Upon completion of the investigation, Department Concerned should conduct a post-incident
review. A report that includes details of actions taken must be produced and given to appropriate
internal audit teams and senior management. This report must include any perceived weaknesses
of the incident response processes, the source of the incident and recommendations for their
improvement. The security incident response plan should be modified and evolved according to
lessons learned and to incorporate industry developments. [Baseline]
