metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J2.1. Policy Objective]
Cloud computing should be managed to ensure adequate security controls are in place to secure the
company assets stored in cloud.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J2.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all business operations of the company. This document pertains all internal
and external cloud services, e.g. cloud-based email, document storage, Software-as-a-Service (SaaS),
Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS), Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS), etc. Personal accounts are excluded.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J2.3. Definition]
Cloud computing refers to the delivery of computing services over the Internet or within a private internal
network. These services primarily involve infrastructure (i.e. servers, storage devices etc.), development
platforms, and software applications.
The cloud refers to numerous data centres managed by third party vendors and located throughout the
world that have installed hardware necessary for the purpose of providing cloud-based solutions
accessible via the Internet.
Cloud computing deployment models:
1. Public cloud: External cloud is defined as an off-premise infrastructure made available over the
Internet which combines the resources of a broad network of users into one or more shared
servers (e.g. Dropbox, Apple iCloud and Microsoft Office 365). A cloud environment that can be
accessible by authorized users.
2. Private cloud: A cloud environment that is managed or owned by an organization on dedicated
and usually on-premises servers that can provide high level control over cloud services and
infrastructure. This can be an appropriate model for highly sensitive data.
3. Community cloud: A cloud computing environment that shared or managed by a specific
community of users from organizations that have shared concerns. This normally involves several
related organizations on dedicated and on-premise servers of their choice and location.
4. Hybrid cloud: Hybrid cloud is comprised of both private and public clouds, allows for certain
components to be hosted by an external party while others remain within the organization’s
control.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.3. Definition > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 126
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 126]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2. Cloud Computing > J2.3. Definition > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 126

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
Cloud computing service models:
1. Software as a Service (SaaS): Capability to use the provider’s applications running on cloud
infrastructure. The applications are accessible from various client devices through a thin client
interface such as a web browser (e.g., Dropbox, Google Drive, and MS O365).
2. Platform as a Service (PaaS): Capability to deploy onto the cloud infrastructure customer-created
or acquired applications created using programming languages and tools supported by the
provider (e.g., Amazon Cloud Service, Microsoft Azure).
3. Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS): Capability to provision processing, storage, networks and other
fundamental computing resources, offering the customer the ability to deploy and run arbitrary
software, which can include operating systems and applications. IaaS puts these IT operations into
the hands of a third party (e.g., Amazon Cloud Service, Microsoft Azure).

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J2.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2.4. Policy Statements > General use of cloud services
Nội dung chính: [General use of cloud services]
1. Use of cloud computing services for work purposes must be formally authorized by the IT Manager
and CIO. [Baseline]
2. A security assessment should be performed prior the appointment of a cloud computing vendor
to assess whether the security, privacy and all other IT management requirements will be
adequately addressed by the vendor. [Baseline]
3. The security assessment should define and document an exit strategy in the event the vendor
proves unable to meet business requirements in future. Support services to be provided by the
vendor to enable a smooth exit should be included in contracts as per Contracting requirements
defined in Terms on Contract Termination under F1.3 and Contractual agreement below.
[Baseline]
4. For any cloud services that require users to agree to terms of service, such agreements must be
reviewed and approved by the IT Manager and CIO, respectively. F1. 3rd Party / Vendor
Management should be followed when selecting and engaging a cloud vendor. [Baseline]
5. The use of such services must comply with the company’s existing Acceptable Use Policy. [Baseline]
6. The use of such services must comply with all laws and regulations governing the handling of
personally identifiable information, corporate financial data or any other data owned or collected
by the company. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2.4. Policy Statements > General use of cloud services

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 127
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 127]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J2.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 127

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
7. The IT Manager/CIO and System Owner decide what data may or may not be stored in the Cloud.
[Baseline]
8. A Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) should be conducted before using cloud services to
process personal data. [GDPR] [LGPD] [PDPO – not mandatory]
9. Security review should be conducted prior to migration to cloud or transfer to other cloud
platform/location to ensure migration process is secure.
10. Legal advice should be sought from Legal Team on country-specific laws when selecting cloud
service location, when applicable. [Baseline]
11. The use of cloud computing services must comply with the entire Information Security Guideline,
including C4. Information Security Classification. [Baseline]
12. Annual security review should be conducted on the security posture and the usage of cloud. A
standard procedure on how to obtain assurance on information security controls implemented
should be defined. Assurance can be obtained by inspecting third party issued certifications that
are required by regulations or independent certifications against standards such as ISO27001,
SOC2 Type II report, ISO27017, ISO27018, PCI Compliance Certificate, listing in CSA Star Registry,
etc. For vendors who do not have third party issued certifications, a security review by
verification of security controls against the Information Security Policy Guidelines or
international standards can be performed. [Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging
Nội dung chính: [J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J3.1. Policy Objective]
Establishing a general policy which outlines the appropriate use of electronic messaging as a means of
communication to prevent the leakage of sensitive information.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J3.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of the company’s staff using electronic messaging and all relevant
messaging systems provided by the company (i.e. Outlook, Skype, Teams etc.).) The Acceptable Use Policy
should be referenced for the usage of public electronic messaging such as WhatsApp or WeChat.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J3.3. Definition]
Electronic messaging such as e-mail and instant messaging (IM) is a type of electronic communication
allowing different personnel or parties to communicate and exchange different types of files and
documents digitally.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J3.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements > Electronic Messaging Account Management
Nội dung chính: [Electronic Messaging Account Management]
1. All e-mail and IM accounts must be protected by a password with reference to the D2. Password
Security. [Baseline]
2. The application and termination process of a corporate e-mail or IM account must be referenced
to the D1. Logical Access Management [Baseline]
3. E-mail address lists or groups must be properly maintained and protected from unauthorised
access and modification. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements > Electronic Messaging Account Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements > Message Confidentiality
Nội dung chính: [Message Confidentiality]
1. Sensitive information sent via electronic means must be well protected based on their
classification with encryption or passwords as referenced to the C4. Information Security
Classification, C5. Information Security Handling, K1. Cryptography and D2. Password Security.
[Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements > Message Confidentiality

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 130
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 130]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J3. E-mail and Electronic Messaging > J3.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 130

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. The company should pre-install, provide the installation file or access link of its official email
system to the staff and all staff must be reminded that they should only use company-authorised
applications to login to their company mailbox. [Baseline]
3. All staff should be reminded that business emails must be sent using their corporate email address.
The use of private email accounts for business purposes should be strictly prohibited and auto-
forwarding outside the corporate email domain shall be blocked unless required for business
purposes and suitably authorised. [Baseline]
4. The e-mail server must be properly configured before connecting to the Internet, covering the
following security settings at a minimum: [Baseline]
o Ports that support or are coupled with encryption should be used;
o Connection security – all e-mail transfer should be encrypted;
o Authentication – only authorised users registered by the company should be able to send
messages;
o Support virus-scan to ensure that the attachments are not corrupted with virus.
o Authentication controls such as Sender Policy Framework (SPF), DomainKeys Identified
Mail (DKIM) or Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, & Conformance
(DMARC) should be implemented for authentication control;
o Access to e-mail record should be restricted to authorised personnel only (i.e. IT Team).
Other access request must be reviewed and approved by senior management.
5. If cloud e-mail service is used by the company, the following security configurations must be
enabled: [Baseline]
o Use dedicated admin accounts (held by the IT Department);
o Multi-factor authentication for all administrative and end user access;
o Encryption at rest;
o Protection against malicious attachments and files;
o Protection against ransomware;
o Protection against phishing attacks.
6. If technically and operationally feasible, information revealing the specific details of the
company’s internal systems or configurations should be avoided in e-mail headers to avoid the
disclosure of system information to external parties. [Advanced]
7. Cardholder data must not be sent in an unprotected format (i.e. without strong encryption) via e-
mail or IM. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Communication with External Parties
Nội dung chính: [Communication with External Parties]
1. E-mails to external parties must have a legal disclaimer appended to them. The e-mail disclaimer
should be developed by the company’s legal counsel and the IT Department should append the
disclaimer to all users within the company. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Communication with External Parties

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 131
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 131]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 131

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. All incoming e-mails including attachments must be virus scanned at the e-mail server or by an
outsourcing service to ensure that they are virus free before being distributed. [Baseline]
3. The URL in all incoming e-mails must be scanned to block malicious URL. [Baseline]
4. E-mails from external parties must be tagged to help users differentiate internal and external
conversations. [Baseline]
5. Click-time protection (i.e. loading the URL in a sandbox environment) should be applied to provide
protection against targeted phishing attacks. [Baseline]
6. E-mail attachments should be tested in a sandbox environment before they can be sent to the
user’s mailbox. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > File Transfer on IM
Nội dung chính: [File Transfer on IM]
1. All staff must be trained not to accept file transfers that they are not expecting as the files might
be infected with malware. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > File Transfer on IM

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E-mail Body Filtering
Nội dung chính: [E-mail Body Filtering]
1. Malicious e-mails commonly include links/URLs to external web sites, some of which may be
disguised (i.e. the text shows an URL, but when clicked, another different URL is retrieved), or
even hidden. The company’s e-mail systems must be able to pre-process e-mail bodies to
neutralise URLs in e-mails or draw attention to their location or real destination. [Baseline]
2. E-mails must be examined by the e-mail system or other vendor solutions to identify whether
they are spam or not (i.e. bulk unsolicited commercial communications or e-mail scams, as distinct
from malicious content). Local policies must be set to prevent spam messages from being
forwarded to individual users’ mailboxes. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > E-mail Body Filtering

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Retention and Removal
Nội dung chính: [Retention and Removal]
1. The retention period for e-mails and IM must be referenced to the A9. Information Retention. All
e-mails and IM must be securely removed and deleted upon the end of their retention period.
The whole process must be documented to ensure accountability. [Baseline]
2. All activity on e-mail servers and the IM control platform must be logged and stored according to
the e-mail and IM retention period. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Retention and Removal

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management
Nội dung chính: [J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J4.1. Policy Objective]
Bring your own device (BYOD) and Mobile Device Management (MDM) can prevent security incidents
such as unauthorised data access and data breaches arising from the usage of various IT devices.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J4.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all devices, including but not limited to, mobile devices, laptop / desktop
computers, and tablet computers, used to access the company’s systems and information. The
registration and disposal of BYOD and company owned devices should be referenced to the C1. Asset
Management and C2. Asset Disposal.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J4.3. Definition]
BYOD refers to the practice that staff are allowed to use their personally owned device for work instead
of being required to use an officially provided company owned device. MDM solutions are applications or
systems that provide a unified console to manage the different types of devices used within an
organization.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J4.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements > BYOD Application
Nội dung chính: [BYOD Application]
1. Except for the systems and portals that are accessible through the public internet, the use of
personal devices for accessing the company’s systems and information must not be permitted,
unless formal request and written approval via an application form has been granted by the
company’s IT Department. [Baseline]
2. The company’s IT management should have the final authority to decide whether a device is
allowed to connect to the company’s IT resources and what type of information is allowed to be
processed, stored and transmitted on the staff-owned devices. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements > BYOD Application

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements > General BYOD Management
Nội dung chính: [General BYOD Management]
1. All staff using their personal devices to access the company’s systems and information will be held
accountable for the devices’ security. Such devices must not be left unattended or lent to anyone
at all times. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements > General BYOD Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 134
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 134]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J4. BYOD & Mobile Device Management > J4.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 134

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
2. The loss of any device containing the company’s information must be reported to IT management
or dedicated contact immediately. [Baseline]
3. Where applicable, BYOD devices must meet the same security requirements as company-owned
devices regarding the password security, anti-malware, encryption, operating system patching
and hardening, firewalls, and the installation of applications and utilities on the device. Relevant
requirements can be referred to the Guidelines listed under the “Reference” section in this
document. [Baseline]
4. The company’s Cyber Security Team should audit the BYOD devices for compliance against the
security requirements listed in the previous statement at least yearly, or if there is evidence of
non-compliance. [Baseline]
5. Users should sign an agreement stating that the BYOD device would be monitored and managed
by the company before that can start to use their device to access the company’s information.
[Baseline]
6. If applicable, lost devices should be remotely wiped (either in their entirety, or just wiping
containers for company information) as soon as possible. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > BYOD & Company Owned Devices Management
Nội dung chính: [BYOD & Company Owned Devices Management]
1. The company retains the right to monitor any corporate services provided. [Baseline]
2. When the users terminate their employment or the mobile device is decommissioned and
disposed of, the company’s IT Team must ensure that all information resources of the company
are removed from the devices, and any copies of the data that have been synchronised onto other
devices are destroyed. [Baseline]
3. The following four fundamental mitigations must be implemented on all mobile devices used to
store/access company information, regardless of the ownership of the device: [Baseline]
o Encrypt - Encryption must be enabled such that sensitive data cannot be recovered from
the device by unauthorized parties. Encryption should extend to any memory cards
inserted into the device;
o Authenticate – Biometric authentication and other reasonable level of authentications
must be enforced on the device;
o Find - ln the event that a mobile device is lost or stolen, various options must be enabled
to locate the device, i.e. using GPS, mobile network location services, Wi-Fi triangulation
etc.;
o Disable - Measures must be in place to ensure that lost or stolen devices can be disabled
remotely. The IMEI of all mobile devices using SIM cards must be recorded by the
company so that network operators can be asked to black-list the device when necessary.
ln addition, vendor-specific services should be used to facilitate remote wiping.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > BYOD & Company Owned Devices Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 135
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 135]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 135

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
4. Jail-broken or rooted devices must not be accepted. [Baseline]
5. Applications should only be accepted and downloaded from official platform-owner-approved
sources. Installation from untrusted sources should be forbidden. [Baseline]
6. The company’s Cyber Security Team should conduct research and list out which operating system
versions for all common mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, Android, iOS, and
Windows) are permitted or not permitted to be used on company owned or BYOD mobile devices,
and also which versions are supported by the company's IT helpdesk or equivalent. The list should
be updated at least annually or whenever new versions are released, and old versions have
reached the end-of-life. [Baseline]
7. MDM system must be used to control and monitor the usage of mobile devices for accessing
company information. The company’s Cyber Security Team should leverage the MDM system for
statement no. 3 listed under “General BYOD Management’ in this document. [Baseline]
8. The MDM solution adopted by the company should be able to perform the followings: [Baseline]
o Monitor the applications and updates being installed on the device;
o Deploy update patches;
o Monitor the usage of devices in the MDM server
o Configure security settings on the device;
o Set up devices that will be used for specific purposes;
o Remote wipe the device;
o Track the device’s location.
9. BYOD mobile device users must consent to having the client portion of MDM software installed
on their devices, as a condition of being allowed to use the device to access company information.
[Baseline]
10. All sensitive information should be removed from the devices after being used immediately.
[Baseline]
11. Apple mobile devices should be backed up only while using the “Encrypt iPhone backup” option
in Apple's iTunes desktop software. [Baseline]
12. Apple mobile devices running on updated iOS may also be backed up using Apple's iCloud as
they are encrypted automatically. 1 [Advanced]
13. Android mobile devices running on Android Pie or newer versions should be backed up to
Google as they are encrypted automatically. [Advanced]
1 Depends on the Apple ID country and region. For details, please check with the company’s Cyber Security Team.

Nội dung chính: [J5. Shadow IT]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J5.1. Policy Objective]
To establish requirements covering the management of shadow IT so as to mitigate multiple IT security
risks such as data leakage, service disruption and breach of regulation.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J5.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of the company’s staff.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J5.3. Definition]
Shadow IT refers to IT systems, devices, software, applications and services used by individual
departments, units or staff under the company without the authorization from the company’s IT
Department.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J5.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.4. Policy Statements > Management of Shadow IT
Nội dung chính: [Management of Shadow IT]
1. The IT Department should develop and maintain a list of approved applications, services and
devices based on the technology needs of the business. All departments must work with IT to
select and implement appropriate tools to meet any new operational technology requirements
and must not use non-approved tools. Relevant security risks and alternative solutions should be
evaluated and considered in the selection, and the approved lists should be circulated to staff.
[Baseline]
2. Appropriate procedures and governance around budgeting, procurement, and reimbursement
should be established to ensure that any IT equipment or services acquired on behalf of staff or
the company are as vetted and approved by IT prior to their acquisition. [Baseline]
3. Continuous network and system monitoring should be performed by the IT Department to
identify new and unknown devices within their network. [Baseline]
4. Log data from firewalls, proxies, security information and event management (SIEM) tools,
network monitoring tools and mobile device management (MDM) tools should be captured to
identify services being used without the consent of the IT Department. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.4. Policy Statements > Management of Shadow IT

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 138
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 138]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J5. Shadow IT > J5.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 138

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
5. A list of high-risk software, applications and services with well-known vulnerabilities should be
identified by the IT Department and they should be blocked using existing infrastructure such as
firewalls, proxies, SIEMs, MDMs and group policies etc. [Baseline]
6. Cloud access security broker (CASB) should be deployed to monitor the corporate network for
unauthorised cloud application usage. [Advanced]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J6.1. Policy Objective]
To raise the security awareness of IoT technology to facilitate secure use of such technology.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J6.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all IoT solutions.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J6.3. Definition]
The Internet of Things (IoT) technology uses network connectivity (e.g. Internet) to interconnect various
physical devices to collect, exchange, process, and react to the data around the physical world, for
example, meter sensors, CCTVs and Smart TVs.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J6.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements > Personal Data Privacy
Nội dung chính: [Personal Data Privacy]
1. Data impact privacy assessment should be performed to determine if sufficient data protection
controls are in place before the implementation of the IoT technology. [GDPR] [PDPO – not
mandatory]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements > Personal Data Privacy

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements > IoT Security
Nội dung chính: [IoT Security]
1. Access to manage IoT technology and the data stored in it should be managed with access control
following the D1. Logical Access Management. [Baseline]
2. Multi-factor authentication should be implemented where possible. [Baseline]
3. For web and mobile applications, the security of the application should be sufficiently tested
against well-established web and mobile security standards (e.g. OWASP) before deployment and
periodically afterwards, following the A3. Vulnerability Management. [Baseline]
4. Strong cryptographic capabilities should be deployed to secure data transfer, in accordance with
the K1. Cryptography. [Baseline]
5. Application should be patched timely in accordance with the A1. Patching. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements > IoT Security

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 140
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 140]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J6. Internet of Things > J6.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 140

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
6. Default accounts and passwords should be changed, unnecessary features should be turned off,
and audit log capability should be enabled. [Baseline]
7. Network infrastructure for IoT should be segregated from business units’ normal computer
network, following the H1. Network Security. [Baseline]
8. IoT asset inventory should be maintained and reviewed in accordance with C1. Asset
Management and disposed of in accordance with the C2. Asset Disposal. [Baseline]
9. IoT data should be backed up in accordance with the A8. Data Backup. [Baseline]
10. Network discovery scanner should be deployed to identify IoT machines in networks. [Advanced]
11. Threat modelling should be conducted as part of the design stage, based on the intended usage
of IoT devices in their operating environment as proposed in the solution stage. [Advanced]
12. Hardware Root-of-Trust should be established for key system components, which include IoT
gateways and IoT platforms. For example, hardware Root-of-Trust can be based on a Trusted
Platform Module (TPM) chip embedded in the device. [Advanced]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media
Nội dung chính: [J7. Social Media]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J7.1. Policy Objective]
Social media use should be managed to prevent unnecessary exposure of company information or any
unauthorised posts that could negatively impact the company’s reputation.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J7.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to the company’s employees managing the corporate social media accounts.
For guidelines regarding the personal use of social media, please refer to the company’s Acceptable Use
Policy.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J7.3. Definition]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.3. Definition > N/A
Nội dung chính: [N/A]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.3. Definition > N/A

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J7. Social Media > J7.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J7.4. Policy Statements]
1. Employees managing the corporate social media accounts must be aware of the effect their
actions may have on the company’s image and reputation. [Baseline]
2. All employees with access to various corporate social media accounts must be documented and
managed with reference to D1. Logical Access Management. [Baseline]
3. All posts on the company’s social media accounts must be authorized and the content of the posts
must not include any confidential or inappropriate information. [Baseline]
4. The company’s Cyber Security team or external service provider should be engaged to
continuously monitor if there are any fraudulent corporate social media accounts across different
platforms on the market. [Baseline]
5. Proper incident response plan should be established with the official social media service
providers to outline the incident handling procedures in case of account hijacking and relevant
contact points from both parties. [Baseline]
6. In case there is unauthorised access or account hijack regarding the corporate social media
accounts, the following actions must be performed: [Baseline]
o Report the incident to the social media service providers;

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence
Nội dung chính: [J8. Artificial Intelligence]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J8.1. Policy Objective]
To raise the security awareness of AI technology to facilitate secure use of such technology in
consideration of the following:
• Privacy: Individual privacy must be respected.
• Fairness and Bias Detection: Unbiased data must be used to produce fair predictions.
• Explainability and Transparency: Decisions or predictions should be explainable.
• Safety and Security: The system needs to be secure, safe to use, and robust.
• Validity and Reliability: Plans must be made to monitor the data and the model.
• Accountability: A person or organization needs to take responsibility for any decisions that are made
based on the model.
• Legal Compliance: Compliance with laws governing AI, data privacy and anti-discrimination, amongst
others, must be observed.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J8.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to the company’s adoption of generative AI (Gen AI) systems (e.g. ChatGPT)
and any AI or machine learning (ML) models or systems company develops internally.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J8.3. Definition]
Artificial intelligence (AI) is the capability of an engineered system to acquire, process and apply
knowledge and skills.
Machine learning (ML) is the process by which a functional unit improves its performance by acquiring
new knowledge or skills or by reorganizing existing knowledge or skills. Machine learning model is a
mathematical construct that generates an inference or prediction, based on input data.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J8.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.4. Policy Statements > Governance and Accountability
Nội dung chính: [Governance and Accountability]
1. Accountability for the outcome of the AI applications should be established. [Baseline]
2. Whether the AI application is developed or purchased, its decisions should be explainable to all
relevant parties. When using immature AI technology that cannot explain decisions, as is the case
for GenAI, procedures should be established to ensure the quality of training data and other
inputs to the models, and to review outputs to detect biased or otherwise unsafe outputs.
Models should be replaced as more mature technologies are released. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.4. Policy Statements > Governance and Accountability

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 144
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 144]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J8. Artificial Intelligence > J8.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 144

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
3. All existing data confidentiality controls and best practices must be in place and observed when
using AI as part of a business process. [Baseline]
4. AI application development should follow G1. System Development Lifecycle. [Baseline]
5. Where third party vendors are relied for the development of AI applications, F1. 3rd Party /
Vendor Management should be followed. [Baseline]
6. Individuals should be informed about the use of AI for automated and AI-assisted decisions.
[Baseline]
7. The option of human intervention should exist if the use of the AI application is assessed to be
of high risk. [Baseline]
8. AI-driven decisions should not discriminate or unintentionally show bias against any group of
users. [Advanced] [PIPL]
9. Privacy regulations and the organizational processes designed to comply with them must be
followed when entering data into the AI system, especially in cases involving a public AI system
(e.g. OpenAI ChatGPT). [Baseline]
10. A Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) should be conducted before using AI technology to
process personal data, and before using personal data for AI development and AI model training
to ensure compliance with data privacy regulations. [Advanced] [GDPR] [LGPD]
11. Data owners must ensure that any planned use of their data in an AI system will be compliant
with this policy and applicable data privacy regulations, and must provide formal approval for
such use. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Training Data Management
Nội dung chính: [Training Data Management]
1. Data should be collected from trusted sources. A list of trusted sources should be kept and
updated. Management approvals for collecting untrusted data should be documented. [Baseline]
2. Data, including AI model and training data, should be classified and protected in accordance with

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Training Data Management

Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > C4. Information Security Classification and C5. Information Security Handling. [Baseline]
Nội dung chính: [C4. Information Security Classification and C5. Information Security Handling. [Baseline]]
3. Version control should be implemented for datasets. [Baseline]
4. Role based access control for AI model and training data used should be enforced by following

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
5. Data should be tracked throughout its lifecycle so that a model’s outputs can be traced back to
the data it was trained on. AI-generated data must be labelled as such so it can be quickly
located if associated data sets must be reviewed, corrected, adjusted, recalled, etc. [Baseline]
6. Data quality should be assessed to ensure data accuracy, completeness, timeliness and
consistency. Identified issues should be remediated in a timely manner. [Advanced]
7. Details about the model's training and functionality must remain strictly confidential. Access to
this information is only granted on a need-to-know basis. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Application Design and Training
Nội dung chính: [Application Design and Training]
1. Production environments should not be used for model design and research. [Baseline]
2. AI model should be trained for robustness. [Baseline]
3. Model selection process should be established. The model selection criteria should be defined
based on quantifiable metrics. [Baseline]
4. Sufficient audit logs should be designed and documented to track the outcome of AI applications
on a continuous basis, so that necessary evidence can be gathered to support investigations
when incidents or unfavourable outcomes arise. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Application Design and Training

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Application and Model Deployment
Nội dung chính: [Application and Model Deployment]
1. Rigorous validation and testing of trained AI models should be performed to confirm the
accuracy and appropriateness of the AI models before they are deployed for production use.
[Baseline]
2. Deployment to production should follow A6. IT Change Control. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Application and Model Deployment

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > On-going Monitoring and Maintenance
Nội dung chính: [On-going Monitoring and Maintenance]
1. AI system data must be audited annually to ensure it has not been tampered with and continues
to meet organizational data-integrity standards. Risks includes privacy, cybersecurity, regulatory
compliance, third-party relationships, legal obligations and intellectual property. For AI systems
that cannot be audited due to proprietary issues, require transparency from vendors by adding
contractual guarantees addressing privacy, confidentiality, security, and that its data will not be
used to train models that are shared with other parties. [Baseline]
2. For AI applications that learn from live data, periodic reviews (e.g., monthly depending on the
risk of the AI application) such as re-validation of the AI model and on-going monitoring should
be conducted to ensure that the application can continue to perform as intended. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > On-going Monitoring and Maintenance

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 146

Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
3. AI systems should be protected against attacks, such as adversarial attacks and data poisoning
that exploit AI models through data manipulation. [Baseline]
4. Contingency measures should be implemented for AI systems and fallback plans should be in
place in case that the AI system is not functioning properly. [Baseline]
• AI models and training data must be backed up in accordance with the A8. Data Backup..
• Recovery time objectives (RTOs) must be tested at least annually.
• Recovery point objectives (RPOs) must be tested at least annually.

Nội dung chính: [J9.1. Policy Objective]
To raise the security awareness of QR code technology to facilitate secure use of such technology.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J9. QR Code > J9.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J9. QR Code > J9.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J9.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to the company’s generation and management of QR code.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J9. QR Code > J9.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J9. QR Code > J9.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J9.3. Definition]
A QR (quick response) code is a two-dimensional barcode that can be used to store data such as website
URLs.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J9. QR Code > J9.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J9. QR Code > J9.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J9.4. Policy Statements]
1. Generated static QR code(s) should be reviewed to check whether it has been replaced with
unknown ones. [Baseline]
2. QR code encryption should be implemented when the QR code should only be readable by
authorized devices. [Baseline]
3. Digital signature should be used for QR code generation to prevent the QR code from being
modified and faked. [Baseline]
4. Dynamic QR codes should be used when the generated QR code is to be used for authentication
or transaction authorization. Validity period of the dynamic QR code should be configured so
that it will become invalid after the specified time. [Baseline]
5. Personal information or other sensitive information should not be stored in QR codes. [Baseline]
6. All outdated or unused QR codes should be disabled. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J9. QR Code > J9.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode
Nội dung chính: [J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J10.1. Policy Objective]
To establish requirements for access to Jardine’s Information systems containing sensitive data. The
standards set in this policy are intended to minimize potential security threats from unauthorized use of
Jardine’s Information systems resource.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J10.2. Scope]
This document applies to all of the company’s staff, contractual/part-time staff, and vendors.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J10.3. Definition]
“Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)” is the method that adds a layer of security that prevents the use of
compromised credentials. The user will be granted access right only after successfully providing multiple
pieces of evidence to an authentication mechanism. The evidence typically has at least two of the
following elements: (1) Something the user knows, (2) Something the user has, (3) Something the user is.
“One-time Passcode (OTP)” is the passcode that provides a security mechanism for logging on to network
or information system resources that can be used once only.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J10.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J10. Multi-Factor Authentication and One-Time Passcode > J10.4. Policy Statements > User Requirements
Nội dung chính: [User Requirements]
1. Register organizational-provided device/ staff-owned device with user credentials to receive the
notification referenced in C. Asset Management. [Baseline]
2. When users attempt to access the JML information system or network resource protected by
multi-factor authentication, “Challenge-response” will be generated as a second authentication
factor. “Physical token,” “One-Time passcode,” and “notification” are examples of the second
factor. For user access to the JML information or network resources, please reference D. Access
control. [Baseline]
3. A compromised credential shall be reported by the user immediately to the responsible parties.
[Baseline]
The following phishing resistant verification methods are available for Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA):
[Baseline]

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
1. Register user credentials and mobile devices which can receive notifications or codes via the
organization-approved authentication app. “Challenge-response” or a six-digit code will be
generated by the authentication app.
2. Biometric MFA bound to a physical authenticator
3. Hardware tokens (e.g., U2F, smartcards)
4. A certificate based authentication that is not based on AD
Phone calls and Text message to smartphones are considered insecure. A plan should be defined with
specified timeline based on the results of a risk assessment to transition to more secure methods.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > MFA system Implementation
Nội dung chính: [MFA system Implementation]
MFA systems are implemented as follows: [Baseline]
1. The MFA system is not susceptible to replay attacks.
2. MFA systems cannot be bypassed by any users, including administrative users unless specifically
documented, and authorized by management on an exception basis, for a limited time period.
3. At least two different types of authentication factors are used.
4. Success of all authentication factors is required before access is granted.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > MFA system Implementation

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > User Registration
Nội dung chính: [User Registration]
The multi-factor authentication requires the user to apply the official registration process to register
organization-provided/ self-owned device(s) and install the authentication app.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > User Registration

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Devices
Nội dung chính: [Devices]
The authentication mobile app can be installed on any supported smartphone or tablet. The approved
authentication app is the preferred solution for JML users. Users should install the app into the
organization-provided/ personally owned device. Jailbroken (IOS) or rooted (Android) devices are
prohibited for use. [Baseline]
The alternative method should be enabled when the user cannot access the mobile devices or
experiencing a bad network connection. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Devices

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Frequency of Challenges
Nội dung chính: [Frequency of Challenges]
The frequency of user challenges depends on the system classification level being protected by MFA; the
responsible IT team should maintain the list of systems and the challenge frequency should be based on
the risk signals or set by IT based on the system classification level with relevant rationale documented.
[Baseline]

The user shall report to the IT department immediately if the registered device is lost, stolen or the user
has reason to suspect their user ID credentials have been compromised. [Baseline]

Nội dung chính: [Log monitoring]
The IT department should be monitoring the access log of the MFA. If any abnormal situation is reflected
in the log, such as unplanned high-frequency off-hours access, unmatched access regional., etc. the
related access right should be suspended for protected data.

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J11. Blockchain > J11.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [J11.1. Policy Objective]
Blockchain should be managed to ensure adequate security controls to secure company assets and data
stored in blockchain.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J11. Blockchain > J11.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J11. Blockchain > J11.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J11.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all Blockchain solutions.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J11. Blockchain > J11.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J11. Blockchain > J11.3 Definition
Nội dung chính: [J11.3 Definition]
Blockchain is a term used to describe distributed ledger technologies (DLT). Cryptographic hash functions
have been applied to this technology. New blocks are added to a blockchain after validating the block’s
integrity by nodes. Blockchains are used to create and maintain a shared system of records and the
platform for tracking transactions or other data.
There are three Blockchain deployment models:
• Public Blockchain has permitted open read access, and anyone can join and write in the network,
such as the Bitcoin blockchain, the Ethereum public blockchain, and other cryptocurrency
blockchains.
• Private Blockchain is the opposite of a public blockchain, and only authorized participants have
read access and can write and join the network. It requires an invitation to join and a set of rules
to determine if someone is fit to join.
• Hybrid Blockchain uses the attributes of private and public chains; the various parties work
together in a closed environment to share data and transactions. The organization can determine
which transaction can remain public or restricted to internal use.
Private Keys are the identity and security credentials in the Blockchain technology which are associated
with financial value.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J11. Blockchain > J11.3 Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
1. Given the emerging trend of the Blockchain, risk assessment (including but not limited to
information security, legal, regulatory, etc) should be conducted prior to the adoption of the
technology and annually after adoption with proper approval from the IT manager /CIO for the
use of Blockchains technology. For information security risk assessment, please refer to A11.
Information Security in Project Management. [Baseline]
2. The use of blockchain technology must comply with the Information Security Guidelines,
including C4. Information security Classification and company’s existing Acceptable Use Policy.
[Baseline]
3. Business Continuity Plan should be established for the blockchain solution. Please refer to E.
Business Continuity Management. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Wallet Management / Key Management
Nội dung chính: [Wallet Management / Key Management]
1. The result of losing or misplacing private keys, may lead to the loss of assets, such as data in the
node, Cryptocurrency in the virtual wallet or NFT. To maintain the operation and protect the
Blockchain’s private keys, key management practice should be adopted. Please refer to K1.
Cryptography. [Baseline]
2. Since a lost private key is non-recoverable by the design of blockchains, the IT manager and CIO
must carefully protect and hold the private key by deploying solution such as hardware security
module (HSM). The hardware security modules (HSMs) provide secure storage to protect the private
key and the mechanism for backup and recovery of the lost or stolen private key. [Baseline]
3. To protect against the theft of private keys used to protect monetary or virtual assets, cryptographic
operations should be implemented to execute within the HSM. [Baseline]
4. Biometric-based signatures should be considered as an option for generating private keys in the
blockchain. This signature applies participant’s biometric information as a private key, such as
fingerprint, face, finger vein. A lost biometric based private key can be recovered through biometrics
at any time. [Advanced]
5. If any third parties are involved into the cryptocurrency operation, multi-signature should be
considered. This may be achieved by splitting access to keys across multiple parties. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Wallet Management / Key Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Secure Coding
Nội dung chính: [Secure Coding]
Secure coding practices should be adopted for blockchain source code (e.g. smart contracts on a
distributed ledger network):
a. Source code management: Since creating smart contracts / distributed ledger network
contracts starts with the cooperation between the business team and developer, the access
right to the source code should be managed and monitored, with timely removal of expired
access with reference to D1. Logical Access Management and D3. Remote Access & Client VPN.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Secure Coding

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 153
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 153]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 153

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
b. Source code compiler: To ensure the source code is associated with the blockchain binary
bytecode, the compilation of the smart contracts / distributed ledger network should be
conducted with reference to A6. IT change Control. The post-verification compilation shall be
created under the same conditions as the original compiler (e.g., the exact same version of
compiler and optimization settings).
c. Source code review: To ensure the security risk of the source code is properly identified, pre-
implementation and regular review should be conducted.
d. Deployment of smart contracts: Once the smart contracts / distributed ledger network code is
deployed to Blockchain, the related coding is unchangeable. Prior to the source code
deployment, code validation should be conducted as such coding cannot be patched and fixed
throughout its entire life cycle.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Network Security and Vulnerability Management
Nội dung chính: [Network Security and Vulnerability Management]
1. A process should be established to manage blockchain network vulnerabilities (e.g., 51% attack,
double-spend attack, malicious smart contracts, denial-of-service (DoS) attack, Sybil attack,
packet sniffing), with reference to A3. Vulnerability Management. [Baseline]
2. The Intrusion detection solution should be in place to protect the data across all involved nodes
from unauthorized modification of data or service disruption. [Baseline]
3. For data stored in the blockchain, the level of authentication and authority assigned for
employee access to a node or nodes on the blockchain network should reference to D1. Logical
Access Management. Access control shall be in place to prevent the access of the non-
authorized user. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Network Security and Vulnerability Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Data Management
Nội dung chính: [Data Management]
1. The CIO or authorised IT Manager shall decide what data may or may not be stored on the
blockchain. [Baseline]
2. The security consideration, management and deployment should be based on the data
classification level and reference to C4. Information Security Classification. Generally, data
classified as “Internal” or above should not be stored in a public blockchain. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Data Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Contractual agreement
Nội dung chính: [Contractual agreement]
1. For contractual requirements for the blockchain service providers (e.g., not allowing vendor
access to customer data; data return or deletion after the termination of blockchains service),
requirements as listed under “Contracting” in the F1. 3rd Party / Vendor Management should be
followed. [Baseline].

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Contractual agreement

content:

Nội dung chính: [J12.1. Policy Objective]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J12. E-Signature Security > J12.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J12. E-Signature Security > J12.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [J12.2. Scope]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J12. E-Signature Security > J12.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J12. E-Signature Security > J12.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [J12.3. Definition]
Electronic signature or e-signature is a legally binding signature signed on electronic documents.
Digital signature is a type of e-signature that is used to validate the authenticity and integrity of a message
by using public key infrastructure.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J12. E-Signature Security > J12.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J12. E-Signature Security > J12.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [J12.4. Policy Statements]
1. E-signature solution should be secured with user authentication, and digital signature technology
on both signature level and document level to prevent tampering of the e-signature and
document contents. [Baseline]
2. Audit trail should be enabled and securely embedded in the document to record the signer, date
and time of each e-signature, and details of any changes made to the document, including how,
when and by whom. [Baseline]
3. Users should consult with the company’s legal counsel and be aware of the applicable regulations
relating to the use of e-signatures to ensure that digitally signed documents will be legally binding.
[Baseline]
4. Formal e-signing procedure should be established. [Baseline]
5. Additional fraud prevention control and compliance measures should be considered and
incorporated into the e-signing procedure. [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > J12. E-Signature Security > J12.4. Policy Statements
