content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography
Nội dung chính: [K1. Cryptography]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.1. Policy Objective
Nội dung chính: [K1.1. Policy Objective]
ln order to secure the company’s sensitive information, encryption should be implemented to provide an
enhanced level of assurance that the data, while encrypted, cannot be viewed or otherwise discovered by
unauthorized parties in the event of data theft, leakage or interception.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.1. Policy Objective

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.2. Scope
Nội dung chính: [K1.2. Scope]
This document is applicable to all of the company’s systems and information. For security controls specific
to network security and VPN, please refer to the H1. Network Security, H2. Wireless Security and D3.
Remote Access & Client VPN.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.2. Scope

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.3. Definition
Nội dung chính: [K1.3. Definition]
Cryptographic controls can be used to achieve different information security objectives, such as
confidentiality, integrity/authenticity, non-repudiation and authentication.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.3. Definition

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.4. Policy Statements
Nội dung chính: [K1.4. Policy Statements]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.4. Policy Statements > Data Encryption
Nội dung chính: [Data Encryption]
1. Only internationally recognised strong and up-to-date encryption algorithms should be used for
protecting sensitive data in transit and at rest. For detailed information regarding the
recommended encryption algorithms, please refer to the Technical Annex – Cryptography.
[Baseline]
2. Certificates used to safeguard data, especially PAN, during transmission over open, public
networks must be confirmed to be valid and not expired or revoked each time a secure connection
is established. [Baseline]
3. Information including data at rest and data in transit must be encrypted to the level required by
the C4. Information Security Classification and C5. Information Security Handling. [Baseline]
4. All backup storage must be encrypted, or password protected with the password strength
referenced to the D2. Password Security. [Baseline]
5. All company laptops’ hard drives must be fully encrypted using only the encryption software
provided by the company. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.4. Policy Statements > Data Encryption

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 158
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 158]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > K1. Cryptography > K1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 158

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
6. All backups transferred to or held by a third party must be encrypted. [Baseline]
7. Encryption algorithms in use must be reviewed at least annually to make sure that there are no
major security vulnerabilities with the company’s encryption control. [Baseline]
8. The company’s Cyber Security team should conduct research and evaluation in selecting the most
suitable encryption methods that meet their business requirements. The followings can be
considered when evaluating different encryption methods: [Advanced]
o Security objectives for storing and/or communicating sensitive information such as:
• Providing confidentiality for stored and/or transmitted data;
• Source authentication for received data;
• Integrity protection for stored/transmitted data;
• Entity authentication, etc.;
o Credible sources such as National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), Federal
Information Processing Standards (FIPS), International Organization for Standardization
(ISO) etc.;
o Whether the encryption method can satisfy all system security, compatibility and
interoperability requirements.

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Key Management
Nội dung chính: [Key Management]
1. All key management activities performed by restricted personnel must be documented to ensure
accountability. The following information should be included: [Baseline]
o Name and signature of the responsible personnel;
o Actions performed;
o Date;
o Systems involved;
o Name and signature of the approver.
2. Default encryption keys must be replaced with a newly generated encryption key during system
installation. [Baseline]
3. All access to the encryption keys must be logged in an audit trail and reviewed at least quarterly.
[Baseline]
4. The master encryption key should not leave the security storage through its service life. [Baseline]
5. Encryption keys in storage, at minimum, must be password protected. [Baseline]
6. For detailed information regarding the encryption key life cycle, please refer to the Technical
Annex – Key Management. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Key Management

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 159
Nội dung chính: [July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 159]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 159

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
7. Encryption keys should be stored securely, i.e. by encrypting them with a key-encrypting key,
placing them within a hardware security module (HSM) or a trusted platform module. [Advanced]
8. Tokenization should be used to substitute unique values for confidential information (e.g. credit
card). [PCI]
9. If key management is not implemented through the usage of an encryption product, or the
company is using manual key management operations, split knowledge and dual control of keys
should be used to eliminate the possibility of one person having access to the whole key. [PCI]
10. Key custodians from the company’s IT Security Operation Team or Application Development Team
should formally acknowledge (in writing or electronically) that they understand and accept their
key custodian responsibilities. [PCI]
11. Encryption keys should be changed whenever personnel with knowledge of the key leave the
company or the role for which the knowledge was necessary and whenever a key is suspected of
or known to be compromised. [Baseline]
12. An inventory of the entity’s trusted keys and certificates used to protect PAN during transmission
should be maintained in order to keep track of the algorithms, protocols, key strength, key
custodians, and key expiry dates. [PCI]
13. The encryption product or solution used by the company should not allow or accept substitution
of keys coming from unauthorized sources or unexpected processes. [PCI]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > L1. Personnel Security > L1.4. Policy Statements > July 2024 Copyright © Jardine Matheson Ltd, 2024 Page 162

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL
Nội dung chính: [JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL]
1. Companies must share the Information Security Policy to all staff and contractors who can access
company data. The staff and contractors must acknowledge at least once every 12 months that
they have read and understood the information security policy and procedures and follow the
Information Security Policy. [Baseline]
2. The formal disciplinary process for employees who commit an information security breach must
be defined by the HR Department. [Baseline]
3. All employees and contractors must be made aware of the procedure for reporting observed or
suspected security incidents to identified management. In addition, users must also report:
[Baseline]
o any observed or suspected security weaknesses in, or threats to, systems or services;
o any unexpected or unexplained behaviour, bugs, or messages that may indicate a breach
of system security has occurred.
4. New staff orientation should contain information security training and be conducted within 60
days following the L2. Security Awareness. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > Termination and Change of Employment
Nội dung chính: [Termination and Change of Employment]
1. Upon the termination of employment or contract, the HR Department must notify the appropriate
parties before the last working date of the employee or contractor in order to fully disable system
and network access for the terminated personnel on or before their last day. [Baseline]
2. The HR Department and the IT Team must work together with the terminating and receiving
managers of the individual changing job roles, to manage the revocation of systems access that
are no longer required and the granting of access to systems that will be required as part of the
job change process. [Baseline]
3. On-going information security requirements and legal responsibilities for a defined period after
the end of the employee’s or contractor’s employment must be included in the employee’s or
contractor’s terms and conditions of employment. [Baseline]
4. Upon termination of employment, all company assets must be returned by the personnel to the
company following the C1. Asset Management. [Baseline]
5. Upon job change, assets no longer required for their job must be returned by the employee to
their previous supervising manager. [Baseline]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > L2. Security Awareness > L2.4. Policy Statements

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > L2. Security Awareness > L2.4. Policy Statements > Frequency
Nội dung chính: [Frequency]
1. Information security awareness training should be an ongoing requirement, at least quarterly. All
staff and contractors should receive security awareness training in information security
appropriate to their position when initially on boarded or transfer to a new position with
substantially different information security requirements. [Baseline]
2. The Security Awareness Program should be: [Baseline]
• Reviewed at least once every 12 months, and
• Updated as needed to address any new threats and vulnerabilities that may impact the
security of the entity’s environment, or the information provided to personnel about their
role in protecting company data.
3. Regular phishing test should be conducted at least annually for all employees. [Baseline]
4. More specific training should be provided to high-risk users (e.g. C-Level executives, staff handling
personally identifiable information or critical systems, privileged users). [Advanced]

metadata.hierarchy: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > L2. Security Awareness > L2.4. Policy Statements > Frequency

content:
Ngữ cảnh: JARDINE MATHESON INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY GUIDELINES CONFIDENTIAL > L2. Security Awareness > L2.4. Policy Statements > Content
Nội dung chính: [Content]
1. Staff awareness training should cover the secure use and protection of removable storage device
and reporting of lost/stolen or suspicious/unknown removable storage device. [Baseline]
2. The information security awareness training should include the following but not limited to
subject areas: [Advanced]
o their roles and responsibilities in relation to information security;
